_**User (2026-01-12 11:53:15Z)**_

refer to @.cursorrules/pense.md and also add support for running voice simulations for type = voice instead of type = chat 

structure of output for each simulation and audios folder has multiple audios - upload all the audios to s3 for that simulation folder and return the path to the directory where it is uploaded

---

_**Agent (composer-1)**_

Reviewing the codebase to understand how simulations are structured and where to add voice type support and S3 audio uploads.

        "originalContents": "\n\n\nclass RunSimulationRequest(BaseModel):\n    type: str = Field(..., description=\"Type of simulation run: 'chat' or 'voice'\")\n\n    @field_validator(\"type\")\n    @classmethod\n    def validate_type(cls, v):\n        if v not in [\"chat\", \"voice\"]:\n            raise ValueError(\"type must be either 'chat' or 'voice'\")\n        return v",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef _recover_simulation_job(job_id: str, details: dict, job_type: str):\n    \"\"\"Recover a simulation job (chat or voice).\"\"\"\n    from routers.simulations import run_simulation_task\n\n    logger.info(f\"Recovering simulation job {job_id} (type: {job_type})\")\n\n    simulation_uuid = details[\"simulation_uuid\"]\n    agent_uuid = details[\"agent_uuid\"]\n    s3_bucket = details[\"s3_bucket\"]\n\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Simulation {simulation_uuid} not found\")\n\n    # Fetch agent\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Agent {agent_uuid} not found\")\n\n    # Fetch personas, scenarios, and metrics\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Simulation {simulation_uuid} has no personas\")\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Simulation {simulation_uuid} has no scenarios\")\n\n    thread = threading.Thread(\n        target=run_simulation_task,\n        args=(job_id, agent, personas, scenarios, metrics, s3_bucket),\n        daemon=True,\n    )\n    thread.start()\n    logger.info(f\"Simulation job {job_id} recovery started\")",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef _recover_simulation_job(job_id: str, details: dict, job_type: str):\n    \"\"\"Recover a simulation job (chat or voice).\"\"\"\n    from routers.simulations import run_simulation_task\n\n    logger.info(f\"Recovering simulation job {job_id} (type: {job_type})\")\n\n    simulation_uuid = details[\"simulation_uuid\"]\n    agent_uuid = details[\"agent_uuid\"]\n    s3_bucket = details[\"s3_bucket\"]\n\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Simulation {simulation_uuid} not found\")\n\n    # Fetch agent\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Agent {agent_uuid} not found\")\n\n    # Fetch personas, scenarios, and metrics\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Simulation {simulation_uuid} has no personas\")\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise ValueError(f\"Simulation {simulation_uuid} has no scenarios\")\n\n    thread = threading.Thread(\n        target=run_simulation_task,\n        args=(job_id, agent, personas, scenarios, metrics, s3_bucket),\n        daemon=True,\n    )\n    thread.start()\n    logger.info(f\"Simulation job {job_id} recovery started\")",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{simulation_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a simulation with personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM simulations command\n    with the agent's config and the simulation's personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Uses the agent linked to the simulation and its LLM model configuration.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Simulation not found\")\n\n    # Get agent from simulation\n    agent_uuid = simulation.get(\"agent_id\")\n    if not agent_uuid:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"No agent linked to this simulation. Link an agent to the simulation first.\",\n        )\n\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Get linked entities\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no personas. Add at least one persona.\",\n        )\n\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no scenarios. Add at least one scenario.\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))",

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{simulation_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a simulation with personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM simulations command\n    with the agent's config and the simulation's personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Uses the agent linked to the simulation and its LLM model configuration.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Simulation not found\")\n\n    # Get agent from simulation\n    agent_uuid = simulation.get(\"agent_id\")\n    if not agent_uuid:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"No agent linked to this simulation. Link an agent to the simulation first.\",\n        )\n\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Get linked entities\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no personas. Add at least one persona.\",\n        )\n\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no scenarios. Add at least one scenario.\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 921
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 767
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/simulations.py"
      },
      "score": 0.38932985067367554
    },

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "@router.post(\"/{simulation_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):\n\n\n    ...\n\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_simulation_job(\n        simulation_id=simulation_uuid,\n        job_type=request.type,\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"simulation_uuid\": simulation_uuid,\n            \"agent_uuid\": agent_uuid,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )\n\n    # Start background task in a separate thread\n    thread = threading.Thread(\n        target=run_simulation_task,\n        args=(job_id, agent, personas, scenarios, metrics, s3_bucket),\n        daemon=True,\n    )\n    thread.start()\n\n    return TaskCreateResponse(task_id=job_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)",

        "originalContents": "\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_simulation_job(\n        simulation_id=simulation_uuid,\n        job_type=request.type,\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"simulation_uuid\": simulation_uuid,\n            \"agent_uuid\": agent_uuid,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )\n\n    # Start background task in a separate thread\n    thread = threading.Thread(\n        target=run_simulation_task,\n        args=(job_id, agent, personas, scenarios, metrics, s3_bucket),\n        daemon=True,\n    )\n    thread.start()\n\n    return TaskCreateResponse(task_id=job_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n\n\n        ...\n\n\n        try:\n            # Build pense config\n            pense_config = _build_pense_simulation_config(\n                agent, personas, scenarios, metrics\n            )\n            model_to_use = pense_config[\"params\"][\"model\"]\n\n            # Create output directory\n            output_dir = temp_path / \"output\"\n            output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n            # Run pense simulation\n            results_prefix = f\"simulations/runs/{task_id}\"\n            result = _run_pense_simulation(\n                model=model_to_use,\n                pense_config=pense_config,\n                output_dir=output_dir,\n                s3_bucket=s3_bucket,\n                s3_prefix=results_prefix,\n                log_prefix=f\"Simulation {task_id}\",\n            )\n\n            # Update job with results\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\n                    \"total_simulations\": result[\"total_simulations\"],\n                    \"metrics\": result[\"metrics\"],\n                    \"simulation_results\": result[\"simulation_results\"],\n                    \"results_s3_prefix\": results_prefix,\n                    \"error\": result[\"error\"],\n                },\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Simulation task {task_id} completed: \"\n                f\"{result['total_simulations']} simulation(s) run\"\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\"error\": f\"Unexpected error during simulation: {str(e)}\"},\n            )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        update_simulation_job(\n            task_id,\n            status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n            results={\"error\": f\"Task failed: {str(e)}\"},\n        )",

        "originalContents": "\n\n        try:\n            # Build pense config\n            pense_config = _build_pense_simulation_config(\n                agent, personas, scenarios, metrics\n            )\n            model_to_use = pense_config[\"params\"][\"model\"]\n\n            # Create output directory\n            output_dir = temp_path / \"output\"\n            output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n            # Run pense simulation\n            results_prefix = f\"simulations/runs/{task_id}\"\n            result = _run_pense_simulation(\n                model=model_to_use,\n                pense_config=pense_config,\n                output_dir=output_dir,\n                s3_bucket=s3_bucket,\n                s3_prefix=results_prefix,\n                log_prefix=f\"Simulation {task_id}\",\n            )\n\n            # Update job with results\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\n                    \"total_simulations\": result[\"total_simulations\"],\n                    \"metrics\": result[\"metrics\"],\n                    \"simulation_results\": result[\"simulation_results\"],\n                    \"results_s3_prefix\": results_prefix,\n                    \"error\": result[\"error\"],\n                },\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Simulation task {task_id} completed: \"\n                f\"{result['total_simulations']} simulation(s) run\"\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\"error\": f\"Unexpected error during simulation: {str(e)}\"},\n            )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        update_simulation_job(\n            task_id,\n            status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n            results={\"error\": f\"Task failed: {str(e)}\"},\n        )",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the LLM simulation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running simulation task {task_id} for agent {agent['uuid']} \"\n            f\"with {len(personas)} persona(s), {len(scenarios)} scenario(s), \"\n            f\"and {len(metrics)} metric(s)\"\n        )\n        update_simulation_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        # Create persistent output directory (not deleted after job completes)\n        output_base_dir = Path(\"simulation_outputs\").resolve()\n        output_base_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        temp_path = output_base_dir / task_id\n        temp_path.mkdir(exist_ok=True)",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the LLM simulation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running simulation task {task_id} for agent {agent['uuid']} \"\n            f\"with {len(personas)} persona(s), {len(scenarios)} scenario(s), \"\n            f\"and {len(metrics)} metric(s)\"\n        )\n        update_simulation_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        # Create persistent output directory (not deleted after job completes)\n        output_base_dir = Path(\"simulation_outputs\").resolve()\n        output_base_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        temp_path = output_base_dir / task_id\n        temp_path.mkdir(exist_ok=True)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def recover_pending_jobs():\n\n    ...\n\n    if pending_simulation_jobs:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Found {len(pending_simulation_jobs)} in_progress simulation job(s) to recover\"\n        )\n        for job in pending_simulation_jobs:\n            job_id = job[\"uuid\"]\n            job_type = job[\"type\"]\n            details = job.get(\"details\")\n\n            if not details:\n                logger.warning(\n                    f\"Simulation job {job_id} has no details, marking as failed\"\n                )\n                update_simulation_job(\n                    job_id,\n                    status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                    results={\"error\": \"Job recovery failed: no details available\"},\n                )\n                continue\n\n            try:\n                if job_type in [\"chat\", \"voice\"]:\n                    _recover_simulation_job(job_id, details, job_type)\n                else:\n                    logger.warning(\n                        f\"Unknown simulation job type: {job_type}, marking as failed\"\n                    )\n                    update_simulation_job(\n                        job_id,\n                        status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                        results={\n                            \"error\": f\"Job recovery failed: unknown job type {job_type}\"\n                        },\n                    )\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Failed to recover simulation job {job_id}: {e}\")\n                update_simulation_job(\n                    job_id,\n                    status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                    results={\"error\": f\"Job recovery failed: {str(e)}\"},\n                )\n    else:\n        logger.info(\"No in_progress simulation jobs to recover\")",

        "originalContents": "\n    if pending_simulation_jobs:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Found {len(pending_simulation_jobs)} in_progress simulation job(s) to recover\"\n        )\n        for job in pending_simulation_jobs:\n            job_id = job[\"uuid\"]\n            job_type = job[\"type\"]\n            details = job.get(\"details\")\n\n            if not details:\n                logger.warning(\n                    f\"Simulation job {job_id} has no details, marking as failed\"\n                )\n                update_simulation_job(\n                    job_id,\n                    status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                    results={\"error\": \"Job recovery failed: no details available\"},\n                )\n                continue\n\n            try:\n                if job_type in [\"chat\", \"voice\"]:\n                    _recover_simulation_job(job_id, details, job_type)\n                else:\n                    logger.warning(\n                        f\"Unknown simulation job type: {job_type}, marking as failed\"\n                    )\n                    update_simulation_job(\n                        job_id,\n                        status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                        results={\n                            \"error\": f\"Job recovery failed: unknown job type {job_type}\"\n                        },\n                    )\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Failed to recover simulation job {job_id}: {e}\")\n                update_simulation_job(\n                    job_id,\n                    status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                    results={\"error\": f\"Job recovery failed: {str(e)}\"},\n                )\n    else:\n        logger.info(\"No in_progress simulation jobs to recover\")",

        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Run Simulation API ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense simulation config from agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config containing system_prompt and llm.model\n        personas: List of persona dicts with description and config (containing gender, language)\n        scenarios: List of scenario dicts with description\n        metrics: List of metric dicts with name and description (for evaluation_criteria)\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from agent config\n    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Build personas list as objects with label, characteristics, gender, and language\n    persona_list = []\n    for persona in personas:\n        persona_config = persona.get(\"config\") or {}\n        persona_obj = {\n            \"label\": persona.get(\"name\", \"\"),  # Store persona name/label\n            \"characteristics\": persona.get(\"description\") or persona.get(\"name\"),\n            \"gender\": persona_config.get(\"gender\", \"female\"),\n            \"language\": persona_config.get(\"language\", \"english\"),\n        }\n        if persona_obj[\"characteristics\"]:\n            persona_list.append(persona_obj)\n\n    # Build scenarios list as objects with name and description\n    scenario_list = []",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n\n                ...\n\n                if transcript_file.exists():\n                    try:\n                        with open(transcript_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                            transcript = json.load(f)\n                    except Exception as e:\n                        logger.warning(\n                            f\"Failed to parse transcript.json for {sim_name}: {e}\"\n                        )\n\n                # Parse config.json to get persona and scenario data\n                persona_data = None\n                scenario_data = None\n                if config_file.exists():\n                    try:\n                        with open(config_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                            config_data = json.load(f)\n                            persona_data = config_data.get(\"persona\")\n                            scenario_data = config_data.get(\"scenario\")\n                    except Exception as e:\n                        logger.warning(\n                            f\"Failed to parse config.json for {sim_name}: {e}\"\n                        )\n\n                simulation_results.append(\n                    {\n                        \"simulation_name\": sim_name,\n                        \"persona\": persona_data,\n                        \"scenario\": scenario_data,\n                        \"evaluation_results\": eval_results,\n                        \"transcript\": transcript,\n                    }\n                )\n\n    # Upload results to S3\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(output_dir):\n        for file in files:\n            local_file_path = Path(root) / file\n            relative_path = local_file_path.relative_to(output_dir)\n            s3_key = f\"{s3_prefix}/{relative_path}\"\n            s3.upload_file(str(local_file_path), s3_bucket, s3_key)\n\n    error = None\n    if result.returncode != 0:\n        error = f\"Command failed: {result.stderr}\"",

        "contents": "      \"assignments\": [],\n      \"tool_call_sound\": null,\n      \"tool_call_sound_behavior\": \"auto\",\n      \"execution_mode\": \"immediate\",\n      \"expects_response\": false,\n      \"response_timeout_secs\": 1,\n      \"parameters\": [\n        {\n          \"id\": \"next_unanswered_question_index\",\n          \"type\": \"integer\",\n          \"value_type\": \"llm_prompt\",\n          \"description\": \"the index of the next unanswered question that should be asked to the user next. don't repeat questions that have been asked before already in the previous responses.\",\n          \"dynamic_variable\": \"\",\n          \"constant_value\": \"\",\n          \"required\": true\n        },\n        {\n          \"id\": \"questions_answered\",\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"description\": \"Optional, the indices of the questions in the full list of questions that have been answered by the user's last response\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"integer\",\n            \"description\": \"the index of each question that got answered by the current response of the user\"\n          },\n          \"required\": true,\n          \"value_type\": \"llm_prompt\"\n        }\n      ]\n    }\n  ], // tools that the voice agent has access to\n  \"personas\": [\n    // array of persona objects for users in the simulation\n    {\n",

        "originalContents": "      \"assignments\": [],\n      \"tool_call_sound\": null,\n      \"tool_call_sound_behavior\": \"auto\",\n      \"execution_mode\": \"immediate\",\n      \"expects_response\": false,\n      \"response_timeout_secs\": 1,\n      \"parameters\": [\n        {\n          \"id\": \"next_unanswered_question_index\",\n          \"type\": \"integer\",\n          \"value_type\": \"llm_prompt\",\n          \"description\": \"the index of the next unanswered question that should be asked to the user next. don't repeat questions that have been asked before already in the previous responses.\",\n          \"dynamic_variable\": \"\",\n          \"constant_value\": \"\",\n          \"required\": true\n        },\n        {\n          \"id\": \"questions_answered\",\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"description\": \"Optional, the indices of the questions in the full list of questions that have been answered by the user's last response\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"integer\",\n            \"description\": \"the index of each question that got answered by the current response of the user\"\n          },\n          \"required\": true,\n          \"value_type\": \"llm_prompt\"\n        }\n      ]\n    }\n  ], // tools that the voice agent has access to\n  \"personas\": [\n    // array of persona objects for users in the simulation\n    {\n",

        "contents": "import os\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nimport traceback\nimport threading\nimport logging\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any\n\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator\n\nfrom db import (\n    create_simulation,\n    get_simulation,\n    get_all_simulations,\n    update_simulation,\n    delete_simulation,\n    get_persona,\n    get_scenario,\n    get_metric,\n    add_persona_to_simulation,\n    add_scenario_to_simulation,\n    add_metric_to_simulation,\n    remove_persona_from_simulation,\n    remove_scenario_from_simulation,\n    remove_metric_from_simulation,\n    get_personas_for_simulation,\n    get_scenarios_for_simulation,\n    get_metrics_for_simulation,\n    get_agent,\n    get_tools_for_agent,\n    create_simulation_job,\n    get_simulation_job,\n    update_simulation_job,\n    get_simulation_jobs_for_simulation,\n)\nfrom utils import (\n    TaskStatus,\n    TaskCreateResponse,\n    get_s3_client,\n    get_s3_output_config,\n)\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/simulations\", tags=[\"simulations\"])\n\n\nclass SimulationCreate(BaseModel):\n    name: str\n    agent_uuid: Optional[str] = None\n    persona_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None\n    scenario_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None\n    metric_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None",

        "originalContents": "import os\nimport json\nimport subprocess\nimport traceback\nimport threading\nimport logging\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional, List, Dict, Any\n\nfrom fastapi import APIRouter, HTTPException\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, Field, field_validator\n\nfrom db import (\n    create_simulation,\n    get_simulation,\n    get_all_simulations,\n    update_simulation,\n    delete_simulation,\n    get_persona,\n    get_scenario,\n    get_metric,\n    add_persona_to_simulation,\n    add_scenario_to_simulation,\n    add_metric_to_simulation,\n    remove_persona_from_simulation,\n    remove_scenario_from_simulation,\n    remove_metric_from_simulation,\n    get_personas_for_simulation,\n    get_scenarios_for_simulation,\n    get_metrics_for_simulation,\n    get_agent,\n    get_tools_for_agent,\n    create_simulation_job,\n    get_simulation_job,\n    update_simulation_job,\n    get_simulation_jobs_for_simulation,\n)\nfrom utils import (\n    TaskStatus,\n    TaskCreateResponse,\n    get_s3_client,\n    get_s3_output_config,\n)\n\nlogger = logging.getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/simulations\", tags=[\"simulations\"])\n\n\nclass SimulationCreate(BaseModel):\n    name: str\n    agent_uuid: Optional[str] = None\n    persona_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None\n    scenario_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None\n    metric_uuids: Optional[List[str]] = None",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 63
          },
          "startPosition": {}
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/simulations.py"
      },
      "score": 0.2825357913970947
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef run_benchmark_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    tests: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    models: List[str],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the benchmark for multiple models in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running benchmark task {task_id} for agent {agent['uuid']} \"\n            f\"with {len(tests)} test(s) and {len(models)} model(s)\"\n        )\n        update_agent_test_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as temp_dir:\n            temp_path = Path(temp_dir)\n\n            try:\n                # Build the base pense config (model will be set per-run)\n                pense_config = _build_pense_config(agent, tests, model=models[0])\n                pense_config[\"params\"] = {}  # Clear model, will be set per-run\n\n                # Create output directory\n                output_dir = temp_path / \"output\"\n                output_dir.mkdir()\n\n                # Run benchmarks for all models in parallel\n                model_results = []\n\n                logger.info(f\"Running {len(models)} models in parallel\")\n\n                with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(\n                    max_workers=len(models)\n                ) as executor:\n                    future_to_model = {\n                        executor.submit(\n                            run_model_benchmark,\n                            task_id,\n                            model,\n                            pense_config,\n                            output_dir,\n                            s3_bucket,\n                        ): model\n                        for model in models\n                    }\n\n                    for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(future_to_model):\n                        result = future.result()\n                        model_results.append(result)\n\n                logger.info(\"Completed running all models in parallel\")",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef run_benchmark_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    tests: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    models: List[str],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the benchmark for multiple models in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running benchmark task {task_id} for agent {agent['uuid']} \"\n            f\"with {len(tests)} test(s) and {len(models)} model(s)\"\n        )\n        update_agent_test_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as temp_dir:\n            temp_path = Path(temp_dir)\n\n            try:\n                # Build the base pense config (model will be set per-run)\n                pense_config = _build_pense_config(agent, tests, model=models[0])\n                pense_config[\"params\"] = {}  # Clear model, will be set per-run\n\n                # Create output directory\n                output_dir = temp_path / \"output\"\n                output_dir.mkdir()\n\n                # Run benchmarks for all models in parallel\n                model_results = []\n\n                logger.info(f\"Running {len(models)} models in parallel\")\n\n                with concurrent.futures.ThreadPoolExecutor(\n                    max_workers=len(models)\n                ) as executor:\n                    future_to_model = {\n                        executor.submit(\n                            run_model_benchmark,\n                            task_id,\n                            model,\n                            pense_config,\n                            output_dir,\n                            s3_bucket,\n                        ): model\n                        for model in models\n                    }\n\n                    for future in concurrent.futures.as_completed(future_to_model):\n                        result = future.result()\n                        model_results.append(result)\n\n                logger.info(\"Completed running all models in parallel\")",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 790
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 579
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/agent_tests.py"
      },
      "score": 0.2818394899368286

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/agent/{agent_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_agent_test(agent_uuid: str, request: RunTestRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run one or more tests for an agent.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM tests command\n    with the agent's config and the combined test cases from all specified tests.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    if not request.test_uuids:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400, detail=\"At least one test UUID is required\"\n        )\n\n    # Verify all tests exist and are linked to the agent\n    tests = []\n    for test_uuid in request.test_uuids:\n        test = get_test(test_uuid)\n        if not test:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} not found\")\n\n        # Verify agent-test link exists\n        link = get_agent_test_link(agent_uuid, test_uuid)\n        if not link:\n            raise HTTPException(\n                status_code=400,\n                detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} is not linked to this agent. Link the test first.\",\n            )\n\n        tests.append(test)\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_agent_test_job(\n        agent_id=agent_uuid,\n        job_type=\"llm-unit-test\",\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"agent_uuid\": agent_uuid,\n            \"test_uuids\": request.test_uuids,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )\n\n    # Start background task in a separate thread\n    thread = threading.Thread(\n        target=run_llm_test_task,\n        args=(job_id, agent, tests, s3_bucket),\n        daemon=True,\n    )\n    thread.start()\n\n    return TaskCreateResponse(task_id=job_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/agent/{agent_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_agent_test(agent_uuid: str, request: RunTestRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run one or more tests for an agent.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM tests command\n    with the agent's config and the combined test cases from all specified tests.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    if not request.test_uuids:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400, detail=\"At least one test UUID is required\"\n        )\n\n    # Verify all tests exist and are linked to the agent\n    tests = []\n    for test_uuid in request.test_uuids:\n        test = get_test(test_uuid)\n        if not test:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} not found\")\n\n        # Verify agent-test link exists\n        link = get_agent_test_link(agent_uuid, test_uuid)\n        if not link:\n            raise HTTPException(\n                status_code=400,\n                detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} is not linked to this agent. Link the test first.\",\n            )\n\n        tests.append(test)\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_agent_test_job(\n        agent_id=agent_uuid,\n        job_type=\"llm-unit-test\",\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"agent_uuid\": agent_uuid,\n            \"test_uuids\": request.test_uuids,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )\n\n    # Start background task in a separate thread\n    thread = threading.Thread(\n        target=run_llm_test_task,\n        args=(job_id, agent, tests, s3_bucket),\n        daemon=True,\n    )\n    thread.start()\n\n    return TaskCreateResponse(task_id=job_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)",

{
  "codeResults": [
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "  ]\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe voice agent is defined in &#96;pense/agent/bot.py&#96;. For now, the default configuration of the bot uses Deepgram for STT, OpenAI for LLM, and Google for TTS. You can configure the bot to use different providers for STT, LLM, and TTS by modifying the &#96;STTConfig&#96;, &#96;LLMConfig&#96;, and &#96;TTSConfig&#96; classes in &#96;pense/agent/bot.py&#96;. We will soon add support for changing the configuration for each provider for each component to override the defaults.\n\nSet the required environment variables for the providers you want to use:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# For the default configuration (Deepgram STT + OpenAI LLM + Google TTS)\nexport DEEPGRAM_API_KEY=your_key\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key\nexport GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/credentials.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense agent simulation -c /path/to/config.json -o /path/to/output\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can use the sample configuration provided in [&#96;pense/agent/examples/simulation/sample_short_english.json&#96;](pense/agent/examples/simulation/sample_short_english.json) to test the voice agent simulation.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense agent simulation -c pense/agent/examples/simulation/sample_short_english.json -o ./out\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nSample output:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n--------------------------------\nRunning simulation simulation_persona_1_scenario_1\nPersona:\nYou are a mother; name is Geeta Prasad, 39 years old lives at Flat 302, Sri Venkateshwara Nilaya, near Hanuman Temple, Indiranagar. Phone number is plus nine one, nine eight triple three, forty-seven twenty-nine zero; never had a stillbirth or a baby who died soon after birth, but has had one baby who died on the third day after delivery; never had three or more miscarriages in a row; in her last pregnancy she wasn't admitted for high blood pressure or eclampsia; only carrying one baby as per the scan; blood group is O positive, so there are no Rh-related issues; did experience light vaginal spotting once during this pregnancy but otherwise no pelvic mass or complications; uncertain about her exact blood-pressure reading, but recalls that it was around 150/95 mmHg at booking; does not have diabetes, heart disease, kidney disease, or epilepsy; does have asthma and uses an inhaler daily; has never had tuberculosis or any other serious medical condition; longer drinks alcohol or uses substances, having stopped completely after learning she was pregnant; is very shy and reserved and uses short answers to questions\nGender: female\nLanguage: english\nScenario:\nthe mother answers all the questions and completes the form, don't ask any further questions after the form is filled\n--------------------------------\n\n[User]: Hi.\nCreating new audio file at out/agent-sim/simulation_persona_1_scenario_1/audios/0_user.wav\n[User (transcribed)]: Hi.\nCreating new audio file at out/agent-sim/simulation_persona_1_scenario_1/audios/1_bot.wav\nAppending audio chunk to out/agent-sim/simulation_persona_1_scenario_1/audios/1_bot.wav\n",

            "text": "The voice agent is defined in &#96;pense/agent/bot.py&#96;. For now, the default configuration of the bot uses Deepgram for STT, OpenAI for LLM, and Google for TTS. You can configure the bot to use different providers for STT, LLM, and TTS by modifying the &#96;STTConfig&#96;, &#96;LLMConfig&#96;, and &#96;TTSConfig&#96; classes in &#96;pense/agent/bot.py&#96;. We will soon add support for changing the configuration for each provider for each component to override the defaults."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 902
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 903,
            "text": "Set the required environment variables for the providers you want to use:"

            "lineNumber": 904

          {
            "lineNumber": 905,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"

            "text": "# For the default configuration (Deepgram STT + OpenAI LLM + Google TTS)"

            "lineNumber": 907,
            "text": "export DEEPGRAM_API_KEY=your_key"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 908,
            "text": "export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 909,

        "contents": "\n\n\ndef run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the LLM simulation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running simulation task {task_id} for agent {agent['uuid']} \"\n            f\"with {len(personas)} persona(s), {len(scenarios)} scenario(s), \"\n            f\"and {len(metrics)} metric(s)\"\n        )\n        update_simulation_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        # Create persistent output directory (not deleted after job completes)\n        output_base_dir = Path(\"simulation_outputs\").resolve()\n        output_base_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        temp_path = output_base_dir / task_id\n        temp_path.mkdir(exist_ok=True)",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the LLM simulation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running simulation task {task_id} for agent {agent['uuid']} \"\n            f\"with {len(personas)} persona(s), {len(scenarios)} scenario(s), \"\n            f\"and {len(metrics)} metric(s)\"\n        )\n        update_simulation_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        # Create persistent output directory (not deleted after job completes)\n        output_base_dir = Path(\"simulation_outputs\").resolve()\n        output_base_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        temp_path = output_base_dir / task_id\n        temp_path.mkdir(exist_ok=True)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "Every user/bot audio turn is persisted inside &#96;\u003coutput_dir\u003e/audios&#96; (see a sample output folder for the exact layout). The terminal also logs each transcript message and every function/tool call issued by the LLM so you can audit the interaction without leaving your shell.\n\n&#96;transcript.json&#96; in the output directory has the full transcript with function calls.\n\nYou can checkout &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/sample_output&#96; as a sample output of the agent conversation which contains all the audio turns and logs from the conversation.\n\n## Speech To Text (STT)\n\nTo evaluate different STT providers, first make sure to organize the input data in the following structure:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n├── /path/to/data\n│   └── stt.csv\n│   └── audios/wav\n│       └── audio_1.wav\n│       └── audio_2.wav\n│   └── audios/pcm16\n│       └── audio_1.wav\n│       └── audio_2.wav\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;stt.csv&#96; should have the following format:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csv\nid,text\naudio_1,\"Hi\"\naudio_2,\"Madam, my name is Geeta Shankar\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nStore the audios in both &#96;wav&#96; and &#96;pcm16&#96; formats as different providers support different formats. The evaluation script will automatically select the right format for the provider you choose to evaluate.\n\nSet the required environment variables for the STT provider you want to evaluate:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# For Sarvam\nexport SARVAM_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For Deepgram\nexport DEEPGRAM_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For OpenAI\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For Google\nexport GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/credentials.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense stt eval -p \u003cprovider\u003e -l \u003clanguage\u003e -i /path/to/data -o /path/to/output -d\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can use the sample inputs provided in [&#96;pense/stt/examples/sample_input&#96;](pense/stt/examples/sample_input) to test the evaluation script.\n\nSample output:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nCreated new user transcript\nStarting new audio streaming: /path/to/audio_1.wav\nFinished streaming audio: /path/to/audio_1.wav\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript\u003e.\n--------------------------------\nCreated new user transcript\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "![Metrics example](images/run_metrics.png)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "Every user/bot audio turn is persisted inside &#96;\u003coutput_dir\u003e/audios&#96; (see a sample output folder for the exact layout). The terminal also logs each transcript message and every function/tool call issued by the LLM so you can audit the interaction without leaving your shell."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 91
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 92,
            "text": "&#96;transcript.json&#96; in the output directory has the full transcript with function calls."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 93
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 94,
            "text": "You can checkout &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/sample_output&#96; as a sample output of the agent conversation which contains all the audio turns and logs from the conversation."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 95
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 96,
            "text": "## Speech To Text (STT)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 97
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 98,
            "text": "To evaluate different STT providers, first make sure to organize the input data in the following structure:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 99
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 100,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 101,
            "text": "├── /path/to/data"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 102,
            "text": "│   └── stt.csv"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 103,
            "text": "│   └── audios/wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 104,
            "text": "│       └── audio_1.wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 105,
            "text": "│       └── audio_2.wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 106,
            "text": "│   └── audios/pcm16"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 107,
            "text": "│       └── audio_1.wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 108,
            "text": "│       └── audio_2.wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 109,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 110
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 111,
            "text": "&#96;stt.csv&#96; should have the following format:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 112
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 113,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;csv"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 114,
            "text": "id,text"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 115,
            "text": "audio_1,\"Hi\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 116,
            "text": "audio_2,\"Madam, my name is Geeta Shankar\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 117,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 118
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 119,
            "text": "Store the audios in both &#96;wav&#96; and &#96;pcm16&#96; formats as different providers support different formats. The evaluation script will automatically select the right format for the provider you choose to evaluate."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 120
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 121,
            "text": "Set the required environment variables for the STT provider you want to evaluate:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 122
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 123,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 124,
            "text": "# For Sarvam"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 125,
            "text": "export SARVAM_API_KEY=your_key"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 126
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 127,
            "text": "# For Deepgram"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 128,
            "text": "export DEEPGRAM_API_KEY=your_key"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 129
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 130,
            "text": "# For OpenAI"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 131,
            "text": "export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 132
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 133,
            "text": "# For Google"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 134,
            "text": "export GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/credentials.json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 135,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 136
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 137,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 138,
            "text": "pense stt eval -p \u003cprovider\u003e -l \u003clanguage\u003e -i /path/to/data -o /path/to/output -d"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 139,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 140
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 141,
            "text": "You can use the sample inputs provided in [&#96;pense/stt/examples/sample_input&#96;](pense/stt/examples/sample_input) to test the evaluation script."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 142
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 143,
            "text": "Sample output:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 144
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 145,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 146,
            "text": "Created new user transcript"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 147,
            "text": "Starting new audio streaming: /path/to/audio_1.wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 148,
            "text": "Finished streaming audio: /path/to/audio_1.wav"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 149,
            "text": "appending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript\u003e."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 150,
            "text": "--------------------------------"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 151,
            "text": "Created new user transcript"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 152,
            "text": "Starting new audio streaming: /path/to/audio_2.wav"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "Every user/bot audio turn is persisted inside &#96;\u003coutput_dir\u003e/audios&#96; (see a sample output folder for the exact layout). The terminal also logs each transcript message and every function/tool call issued by the LLM so you can audit the interaction without leaving your shell.\n\n&#96;transcript.json&#96; in the output directory has the full transcript with function calls.\n\nYou can checkout &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/sample_output&#96; as a sample output of the agent conversation which contains all the audio turns and logs from the conversation.\n\n## Speech To Text (STT)\n\nTo evaluate different STT providers, first make sure to organize the input data in the following structure:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n├── /path/to/data\n│   └── stt.csv\n│   └── audios/wav\n│       └── audio_1.wav\n│       └── audio_2.wav\n│   └── audios/pcm16\n│       └── audio_1.wav\n│       └── audio_2.wav\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;stt.csv&#96; should have the following format:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csv\nid,text\naudio_1,\"Hi\"\naudio_2,\"Madam, my name is Geeta Shankar\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nStore the audios in both &#96;wav&#96; and &#96;pcm16&#96; formats as different providers support different formats. The evaluation script will automatically select the right format for the provider you choose to evaluate.\n\nSet the required environment variables for the STT provider you want to evaluate:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# For Sarvam\nexport SARVAM_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For Deepgram\nexport DEEPGRAM_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For OpenAI\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For Google\nexport GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS=/path/to/credentials.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense stt eval -p \u003cprovider\u003e -l \u003clanguage\u003e -i /path/to/data -o /path/to/output -d\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nYou can use the sample inputs provided in [&#96;pense/stt/examples/sample_input&#96;](pense/stt/examples/sample_input) to test the evaluation script.\n\nSample output:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nCreated new user transcript\nStarting new audio streaming: /path/to/audio_1.wav\nFinished streaming audio: /path/to/audio_1.wav\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript\u003e.\n--------------------------------\nCreated new user transcript\n",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 152
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 87
          }

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n\n                ...\n\n                if transcript_file.exists():\n                    try:\n                        with open(transcript_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                            transcript = json.load(f)\n                    except Exception as e:\n                        logger.warning(\n                            f\"Failed to parse transcript.json for {sim_name}: {e}\"\n                        )\n\n                # Parse config.json to get persona and scenario data\n                persona_data = None\n                scenario_data = None\n                if config_file.exists():\n                    try:\n                        with open(config_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                            config_data = json.load(f)\n                            persona_data = config_data.get(\"persona\")\n                            scenario_data = config_data.get(\"scenario\")\n                    except Exception as e:\n                        logger.warning(\n                            f\"Failed to parse config.json for {sim_name}: {e}\"\n                        )\n\n                simulation_results.append(\n                    {\n                        \"simulation_name\": sim_name,\n                        \"persona\": persona_data,\n                        \"scenario\": scenario_data,\n                        \"evaluation_results\": eval_results,\n                        \"transcript\": transcript,\n                    }\n                )\n\n    # Upload results to S3\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(output_dir):\n        for file in files:\n            local_file_path = Path(root) / file\n            relative_path = local_file_path.relative_to(output_dir)\n            s3_key = f\"{s3_prefix}/{relative_path}\"\n            s3.upload_file(str(local_file_path), s3_bucket, s3_key)\n\n    error = None\n    if result.returncode != 0:\n        error = f\"Command failed: {result.stderr}\"",

        "originalContents": "\n                if transcript_file.exists():\n                    try:\n                        with open(transcript_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                            transcript = json.load(f)\n                    except Exception as e:\n                        logger.warning(\n                            f\"Failed to parse transcript.json for {sim_name}: {e}\"\n                        )\n\n                # Parse config.json to get persona and scenario data\n                persona_data = None\n                scenario_data = None\n                if config_file.exists():\n                    try:\n                        with open(config_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                            config_data = json.load(f)\n                            persona_data = config_data.get(\"persona\")\n                            scenario_data = config_data.get(\"scenario\")\n                    except Exception as e:\n                        logger.warning(\n                            f\"Failed to parse config.json for {sim_name}: {e}\"\n                        )\n\n                simulation_results.append(\n                    {\n                        \"simulation_name\": sim_name,\n                        \"persona\": persona_data,\n                        \"scenario\": scenario_data,\n                        \"evaluation_results\": eval_results,\n                        \"transcript\": transcript,\n                    }\n                )\n\n    # Upload results to S3\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(output_dir):\n        for file in files:\n            local_file_path = Path(root) / file\n            relative_path = local_file_path.relative_to(output_dir)\n            s3_key = f\"{s3_prefix}/{relative_path}\"\n            s3.upload_file(str(local_file_path), s3_bucket, s3_key)\n\n    error = None\n    if result.returncode != 0:\n        error = f\"Command failed: {result.stderr}\"",

        "contents": "def run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n\n\n        ...\n\n\n        try:\n            # Build pense config\n            pense_config = _build_pense_simulation_config(\n                agent, personas, scenarios, metrics\n            )\n            model_to_use = pense_config[\"params\"][\"model\"]\n\n            # Create output directory\n            output_dir = temp_path / \"output\"\n            output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n            # Run pense simulation\n            results_prefix = f\"simulations/runs/{task_id}\"\n            result = _run_pense_simulation(\n                model=model_to_use,\n                pense_config=pense_config,\n                output_dir=output_dir,\n                s3_bucket=s3_bucket,\n                s3_prefix=results_prefix,\n                log_prefix=f\"Simulation {task_id}\",\n            )\n\n            # Update job with results\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\n                    \"total_simulations\": result[\"total_simulations\"],\n                    \"metrics\": result[\"metrics\"],\n                    \"simulation_results\": result[\"simulation_results\"],\n                    \"results_s3_prefix\": results_prefix,\n                    \"error\": result[\"error\"],\n                },\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Simulation task {task_id} completed: \"\n                f\"{result['total_simulations']} simulation(s) run\"\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\"error\": f\"Unexpected error during simulation: {str(e)}\"},\n            )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        update_simulation_job(\n            task_id,\n            status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n            results={\"error\": f\"Task failed: {str(e)}\"},\n        )",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "lineNumber": 671,
            "text": "    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\""

            "lineNumber": 672,
            "text": "    results_file = output_dir / \"results.csv\""

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"",

        "contents": "\n\n\ndef run_evaluation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    request: STTEvaluationRequest,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the STT evaluation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running evaluation task {task_id} with {len(request.providers)} providers\"\n        )\n        update_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        # Create temporary directory for processing\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as temp_dir:\n            temp_path = Path(temp_dir)\n\n            try:\n                # Create directory structure\n                input_dir = temp_path / \"input\"\n                input_dir.mkdir()\n                audios_wav_dir = input_dir / \"audios\" / \"wav\"\n                audios_pcm16_dir = input_dir / \"audios\" / \"pcm16\"\n                audios_wav_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n                audios_pcm16_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n\n                # Download audio files from S3 and create CSV\n                stt_csv_path = input_dir / \"stt.csv\"",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef run_evaluation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    request: STTEvaluationRequest,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the STT evaluation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running evaluation task {task_id} with {len(request.providers)} providers\"\n        )\n        update_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        # Create temporary directory for processing\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as temp_dir:\n            temp_path = Path(temp_dir)\n\n            try:\n                # Create directory structure\n                input_dir = temp_path / \"input\"\n                input_dir.mkdir()\n                audios_wav_dir = input_dir / \"audios\" / \"wav\"\n                audios_pcm16_dir = input_dir / \"audios\" / \"pcm16\"\n                audios_wav_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n                audios_pcm16_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n\n                # Download audio files from S3 and create CSV\n                stt_csv_path = input_dir / \"stt.csv\"",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def evaluate_tts_provider(\n    run_id: str,\n    provider: str,\n    language: str,\n    input_csv: Path,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    port: int,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n) -\u003e ProviderResult:\n\n\n        ...\n\n\n        if results_file.exists():\n            results_data = []\n            with open(results_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n                for row in reader:\n                    results_data.append(dict(row))\n\n        # Upload all files including generated audio files\n        # Map local audio paths to S3 keys for presigned URL generation\n        audio_files_uploaded = 0\n        audio_path_to_s3_key = {}\n        for root, dirs, files in os.walk(provider_output_dir):\n            for file in files:\n                local_file_path = Path(root) / file\n                relative_path = local_file_path.relative_to(provider_output_dir)\n                s3_key = f\"{results_prefix}/{relative_path}\"\n\n                # Check if this is an audio file\n                is_audio_file = (\n                    file.endswith(\".wav\")\n                    or file.endswith(\".mp3\")\n                    or file.endswith(\".ogg\")\n                    or \"audios\" in str(relative_path).lower()\n                )\n\n                s3.upload_file(str(local_file_path), s3_bucket, s3_key)\n\n                if is_audio_file:\n                    audio_files_uploaded += 1\n                    # Store mapping from local path to S3 key\n                    audio_path_to_s3_key[str(local_file_path)] = s3_key\n                    logger.info(f\"Uploaded audio file {file} to S3: {s3_key}\")\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Uploaded {audio_files_uploaded} audio file(s) for provider {provider}\"\n        )",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "Starting new audio streaming: /path/to/audio_2.wav\nFinished streaming audio: /path/to/audio_2.wav\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript_part_1\u003e,\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript_part_2\u003e,\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe output of the evaluation script will be saved in the output directory.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n/path/to/output\n├── sarvam_english\n│   ├── logs\n│   ├── results.log\n│   ├── results.csv\n│   └── metrics.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;results.csv&#96; will have the following columns:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csv\nid,gt,pred,wer,string_similarity,llm_judge_score,llm_judge_reasoning\n3_1_english_baseline,\"Please write Rekha Kumari, sister.\", Please write Reha Kumari's sister.,0.4,0.927536231884058,False,\"The source says 'Rekha Kumari, sister' which indicates the name is 'Rekha Kumari' and she is a sister. The transcription says 'Reha Kumari's sister', which changes the name to 'Reha Kumari' and refers to her sister, not Rekha Kumari herself. The name is different ('Rekha' vs 'Reha') and the relationship is also changed (from identifying Rekha Kumari as the sister to referring to the sister of Reha Kumari). Therefore, the values do not match.\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe definition of all the metrics we compute are stored in [&#96;pense/stt/metrics.py&#96;](pense/stt/metrics.py).\n\n&#96;metrics.json&#96; will have the following format:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n[\n  {\n    \"wer\": 0.12962962962962962 // mean of the Word Error Rate (WER) across all audio files\n  },\n",

        "originalContents": "Starting new audio streaming: /path/to/audio_2.wav\nFinished streaming audio: /path/to/audio_2.wav\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript_part_1\u003e,\nappending to last user transcript: \u003ctranscript_part_2\u003e,\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe output of the evaluation script will be saved in the output directory.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n/path/to/output\n├── sarvam_english\n│   ├── logs\n│   ├── results.log\n│   ├── results.csv\n│   └── metrics.json\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;results.csv&#96; will have the following columns:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csv\nid,gt,pred,wer,string_similarity,llm_judge_score,llm_judge_reasoning\n3_1_english_baseline,\"Please write Rekha Kumari, sister.\", Please write Reha Kumari's sister.,0.4,0.927536231884058,False,\"The source says 'Rekha Kumari, sister' which indicates the name is 'Rekha Kumari' and she is a sister. The transcription says 'Reha Kumari's sister', which changes the name to 'Reha Kumari' and refers to her sister, not Rekha Kumari herself. The name is different ('Rekha' vs 'Reha') and the relationship is also changed (from identifying Rekha Kumari as the sister to referring to the sister of Reha Kumari). Therefore, the values do not match.\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThe definition of all the metrics we compute are stored in [&#96;pense/stt/metrics.py&#96;](pense/stt/metrics.py).\n\n&#96;metrics.json&#96; will have the following format:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n[\n  {\n    \"wer\": 0.12962962962962962 // mean of the Word Error Rate (WER) across all audio files\n  },\n",
