        "contents": "def run_evaluation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    request: STTEvaluationRequest,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n\n                ...\n\n                with open(stt_csv_path, \"w\", newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as csvfile:\n                    writer = csv.writer(csvfile)\n                    writer.writerow([\"id\", \"text\"])\n\n                    for idx, (audio_path, gt_text) in enumerate(\n                        zip(request.audio_paths, request.texts)\n                    ):\n                        # Parse S3 path (format: s3://bucket/key or bucket/key)\n                        if audio_path.startswith(\"s3://\"):\n                            parts = audio_path[5:].split(\"/\", 1)\n                            bucket = parts[0]\n                            key = parts[1] if len(parts) \u003e 1 else \"\"\n                        else:\n                            parts = audio_path.split(\"/\", 1)\n                            bucket = parts[0]\n                            key = parts[1] if len(parts) \u003e 1 else \"\"\n\n                        # Generate audio ID\n                        audio_id = f\"audio_{idx + 1}\"\n\n                        # Download audio file\n                        local_wav_path = audios_wav_dir / f\"{audio_id}.wav\"\n                        local_pcm16_path = audios_pcm16_dir / f\"{audio_id}.wav\"\n\n                        logger.info(\n                            f\"Downloading audio file from {bucket}/{key} to {local_wav_path}\"\n                        )\n                        s3.download_file(bucket, key, str(local_wav_path))\n\n                        # Convert to pcm16 (mono, 16KHz, s16le) using ffmpeg instead of copying\n                        cmd = [\n                            \"ffmpeg\",\n                            \"-y\",\n                            \"-i\",\n                            str(local_wav_path),\n                            \"-ac\",\n                            \"1\",\n                            \"-ar\",\n                            \"16000\",\n                            \"-f\",\n                            \"wav\",\n                            \"-sample_fmt\",\n                            \"s16\",\n                            str(local_pcm16_path),\n                        ]",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "      \"characteristics\": \"description of user personality, background, and behavior\",\n      \"gender\": \"female\", // gender of the persona (used in user system prompt)\n      \"language\": \"english\", // language for both agent and user in the simulation\n      \"interruption_sensitivity\": \"medium\" // interruption sensitivity: \"none\", \"low\", \"medium\", or \"high\" (maps to probabilities: 0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.8)\n    }\n  ], // different user personas to evaluate for the voice agent\n  \"scenarios\": [\n    // array of scenario objects to be run with each user persona\n    {\n      \"description\": \"description of the scenario to be played out\"\n    }\n  ], // the different scenarios to evaluate for each persona\n  \"evaluation_criteria\": [\n    // array of criteria to evaluate each simulation against\n    {\n      \"name\": \"question_completeness\", // unique name for the criterion\n      \"description\": \"Whether all the questions in the form were covered in the conversation\" // description used by LLM judge to evaluate\n    }\n",

            "lineNumber": 901,
            "text": "The voice agent is defined in &#96;pense/agent/bot.py&#96;. For now, the default configuration of the bot uses Deepgram for STT, OpenAI for LLM, and Google for TTS. You can configure the bot to use different providers for STT, LLM, and TTS by modifying the &#96;STTConfig&#96;, &#96;LLMConfig&#96;, and &#96;TTSConfig&#96; classes in &#96;pense/agent/bot.py&#96;. {
  "codeResults": [
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@app.post(\"/presigned-url\", response_model=PresignedURLResponse)\nasync def get_presigned_url(request: PresignedURLRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Generate a presigned URL for uploading files to S3.\n\n    The file will be stored at: bucket/task_type/media/UUID.extension\n\n    Args:\n        request: Contains task_type (stt, tts, agent) and file_extension\n\n    Returns:\n        Presigned URL, S3 path, and expiration time\n    \"\"\"\n    # Validate file extension (remove leading dot if present)\n    file_extension = request.extension\n    if not file_extension:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"File extension cannot be empty\",\n        )\n\n    # Validate task type\n    if request.task_type not in [\"stt\", \"tts\", \"agent\"]:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"task_type must be one of: stt, tts, agent\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 bucket from environment\n    s3_bucket = os.getenv(\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET\")\n    if not s3_bucket:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=500,\n            detail=\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET environment variable is required\",\n        )\n\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Generate UUID for unique file name\n    file_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Construct S3 key: task_type/media/UUID.extension (no prefix)\n    s3_key = f\"{request.task_type}/media/{file_uuid}.{file_extension}\"\n\n    # Generate presigned URL (expires in 1 hour)\n    expiration = 3600  # 1 hour in seconds\n\n    presigned_url = s3.generate_presigned_url(\n        \"put_object\",\n        Params={\n            \"Bucket\": s3_bucket,\n            \"Key\": s3_key,\n            \"ContentType\": request.content_type,\n        },\n        ExpiresIn=expiration,\n    )\n\n    return PresignedURLResponse(\n        presigned_url=presigned_url,\n        s3_path=f\"s3://{s3_bucket}/{s3_key}\",\n        expires_in=expiration,\n    )",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@app.post(\"/presigned-url\", response_model=PresignedURLResponse)\nasync def get_presigned_url(request: PresignedURLRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Generate a presigned URL for uploading files to S3.\n\n    The file will be stored at: bucket/task_type/media/UUID.extension\n\n    Args:\n        request: Contains task_type (stt, tts, agent) and file_extension\n\n    Returns:\n        Presigned URL, S3 path, and expiration time\n    \"\"\"\n    # Validate file extension (remove leading dot if present)\n    file_extension = request.extension\n    if not file_extension:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"File extension cannot be empty\",\n        )\n\n    # Validate task type\n    if request.task_type not in [\"stt\", \"tts\", \"agent\"]:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"task_type must be one of: stt, tts, agent\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 bucket from environment\n    s3_bucket = os.getenv(\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET\")\n    if not s3_bucket:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=500,\n            detail=\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET environment variable is required\",\n        )\n\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Generate UUID for unique file name\n    file_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Construct S3 key: task_type/media/UUID.extension (no prefix)\n    s3_key = f\"{request.task_type}/media/{file_uuid}.{file_extension}\"\n\n    # Generate presigned URL (expires in 1 hour)\n    expiration = 3600  # 1 hour in seconds\n\n    presigned_url = s3.generate_presigned_url(\n        \"put_object\",\n        Params={\n            \"Bucket\": s3_bucket,\n            \"Key\": s3_key,\n            \"ContentType\": request.content_type,\n        },\n        ExpiresIn=expiration,\n    )\n\n    return PresignedURLResponse(\n        presigned_url=presigned_url,\n        s3_path=f\"s3://{s3_bucket}/{s3_key}\",\n        expires_in=expiration,\n    )",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def evaluate_tts_provider(\n    run_id: str,\n    provider: str,\n    language: str,\n    input_csv: Path,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    port: int,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n) -\u003e ProviderResult:\n\n\n        ...\n\n\n        if results_file.exists():\n            results_data = []\n            with open(results_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n                for row in reader:\n                    results_data.append(dict(row))\n\n        # Upload all files including generated audio files\n        # Map local audio paths to S3 keys for presigned URL generation\n        audio_files_uploaded = 0\n        audio_path_to_s3_key = {}\n        for root, dirs, files in os.walk(provider_output_dir):\n            for file in files:\n                local_file_path = Path(root) / file\n                relative_path = local_file_path.relative_to(provider_output_dir)\n                s3_key = f\"{results_prefix}/{relative_path}\"\n\n                # Check if this is an audio file\n                is_audio_file = (\n                    file.endswith(\".wav\")\n                    or file.endswith(\".mp3\")\n                    or file.endswith(\".ogg\")\n                    or \"audios\" in str(relative_path).lower()\n                )\n\n                s3.upload_file(str(local_file_path), s3_bucket, s3_key)\n\n                if is_audio_file:\n                    audio_files_uploaded += 1\n                    # Store mapping from local path to S3 key\n                    audio_path_to_s3_key[str(local_file_path)] = s3_key\n                    logger.info(f\"Uploaded audio file {file} to S3: {s3_key}\")\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Uploaded {audio_files_uploaded} audio file(s) for provider {provider}\"\n        )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,

            "text": "def evaluate_tts_provider("

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n        if results_file.exists():\n            results_data = []\n            with open(results_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n                for row in reader:\n                    results_data.append(dict(row))\n\n        # Upload all files including generated audio files\n        # Map local audio paths to S3 keys for presigned URL generation\n        audio_files_uploaded = 0\n        audio_path_to_s3_key = {}\n        for root, dirs, files in os.walk(provider_output_dir):\n            for file in files:\n                local_file_path = Path(root) / file\n                relative_path = local_file_path.relative_to(provider_output_dir)\n                s3_key = f\"{results_prefix}/{relative_path}\"\n\n                # Check if this is an audio file\n                is_audio_file = (\n                    file.endswith(\".wav\")\n                    or file.endswith(\".mp3\")\n                    or file.endswith(\".ogg\")\n                    or \"audios\" in str(relative_path).lower()\n                )\n\n                s3.upload_file(str(local_file_path), s3_bucket, s3_key)\n\n                if is_audio_file:\n                    audio_files_uploaded += 1\n                    # Store mapping from local path to S3 key\n                    audio_path_to_s3_key[str(local_file_path)] = s3_key\n                    logger.info(f\"Uploaded audio file {file} to S3: {s3_key}\")\n\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Uploaded {audio_files_uploaded} audio file(s) for provider {provider}\"\n        )",

        "contents": "def evaluate_provider(\n    run_id: str,\n    provider: str,\n    language: str,\n    input_dir: Path,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    port: int,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n) -\u003e ProviderResult:\n\n\n        ...\n\n\n        for root, dirs, files in os.walk(provider_output_dir):\n            for file in files:\n                local_file_path = Path(root) / file\n                relative_path = local_file_path.relative_to(provider_output_dir)\n                s3_key = f\"{results_prefix}/{relative_path}\"\n\n                s3.upload_file(str(local_file_path), s3_bucket, s3_key)\n\n        return ProviderResult(\n            provider=provider,\n            success=True,\n            message=f\"STT evaluation completed successfully for {provider}\",\n            metrics=metrics_data,\n            results=results_data,\n        )\n\n    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        return ProviderResult(\n            provider=provider,\n            success=False,\n            message=f\"STT eval failed: {e.stderr}\",\n        )\n    except Exception as e:\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        return ProviderResult(\n            provider=provider,\n            success=False,\n            message=f\"Unexpected error: {str(e)}\",\n        )",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def _run_pense_test(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM test\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n\n\n    ...\n\n\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(output_dir):\n        for file in files:\n            file_path = Path(root) / file\n            if file == \"results.json\" and results_data is None:\n                with open(file_path, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                    results_data = json.load(f)\n            elif file == \"metrics.json\" and metrics_data is None:\n                with open(file_path, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                    metrics_data = json.load(f)\n\n    # Upload results to S3\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(output_dir):\n        for file in files:\n            local_file_path = Path(root) / file\n            relative_path = local_file_path.relative_to(output_dir)\n            s3_key = f\"{s3_prefix}/{relative_path}\"\n            s3.upload_file(str(local_file_path), s3_bucket, s3_key)\n\n    # Parse metrics\n    total_tests = 0\n    passed = 0\n    failed = 0\n\n    if metrics_data and isinstance(metrics_data, dict):\n        total_tests = metrics_data.get(\"total\", 0)\n        passed = metrics_data.get(\"passed\", 0)\n        failed = total_tests - passed\n\n    # Parse results\n    test_results = []",

        "contents": "\n\n\ndef evaluate_provider(\n    run_id: str,\n    provider: str,\n    language: str,\n    input_dir: Path,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    port: int,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n) -\u003e ProviderResult:\n    \"\"\"Evaluate a single STT provider.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        # Run pense STT eval command\n        eval_cmd = [\n            \"pense\",\n            \"stt\",\n            \"eval\",\n            \"-p\",\n            provider,\n            \"-l\",\n            language,\n            \"-i\",\n            str(input_dir),\n            \"-o\",\n            str(output_dir),\n            \"--port\",\n            str(port),\n        ]\n\n        logger.info(f\"Running {run_id} with command: {' '.join(eval_cmd)}\")\n\n        subprocess.run(\n            eval_cmd,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            check=True,\n            cwd=str(output_dir.parent),\n        )\n\n        # Find the provider-specific output directory\n        provider_output_dir = None\n        for item in output_dir.iterdir():\n            if item.is_dir() and provider in item.name.lower():\n                provider_output_dir = item\n                break\n\n        # Upload STT eval results to S3\n        results_prefix = f\"stt/evals/{run_id}/outputs/{provider}\"\n\n        # Read metrics.json and results.csv before uploading\n        metrics_data = None\n        results_data = None\n\n        metrics_file = provider_output_dir / \"metrics.json\"\n        results_file = provider_output_dir / \"results.csv\"\n\n        if metrics_file.exists():\n            with open(metrics_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                metrics_data = json.load(f)\n\n        if results_file.exists():\n            results_data = []\n            with open(results_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                reader = csv.DictReader(f)\n                for row in reader:\n                    results_data.append(dict(row))",

Run pense STT eval command\n        eval_cmd = [\n            \"pense\",\n            \"stt\",\n            \"eval\",\n            \"-p\",\n            provider,\n            \"-l\",\n            language,\n            \"-i\",\n            str(input_dir),\n            \"-o\",\n            str(output_dir),\n            \"--port\",\n            str(port),\n        ]\n\n        logger.info(f\"Running {run_id} with command: {' '.join(eval_cmd)}\")\n\n        subprocess.run(\n            eval_cmd,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            check=True,\n            cwd=str(output_dir.parent),\n        )\n\n        #         "contents": "\n\n\ndef get_s3_output_config():\n    \"\"\"Get S3 output configuration from environment variables.\"\"\"\n    bucket = os.getenv(\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET\")\n\n    if not bucket:\n        raise ValueError(\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET environment variable is required\")\n\n    return bucket",

          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef get_s3_output_config():\n    \"\"\"Get S3 output configuration from environment variables.\"\"\"\n    bucket = os.getenv(\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET\")\n\n    if not bucket:\n        raise ValueError(\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET environment variable is required\")\n\n    return bucket",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 522,

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {

          },

          }

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef evaluate_tts_provider(\n    run_id: str,\n    provider: str,\n    language: str,\n    input_csv: Path,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    port: int,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n) -\u003e ProviderResult:\n    \"\"\"Evaluate a single TTS provider.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        # Run pense TTS eval command\n        eval_cmd = [\n            \"pense\",\n            \"tts\",\n            \"eval\",\n            \"-p\",\n            provider,\n            \"-l\",\n            language,\n            \"-i\",\n            str(input_csv),\n            \"-o\",\n            str(output_dir),\n            \"--port\",\n            str(port),\n        ]\n\n        logger.info(f\"Running TTS eval {run_id} with command: {' '.join(eval_cmd)}\")\n\n        subprocess.run(\n            eval_cmd,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            check=True,\n            cwd=str(output_dir.parent),\n        )\n\n        # Find the provider-specific output directory\n        provider_output_dir = None\n        for item in output_dir.iterdir():\n            if item.is_dir() and provider in item.name.lower():\n                provider_output_dir = item\n                break\n\n        if provider_output_dir is None:\n            # Try to find any directory in output_dir\n            dirs = [d for d in output_dir.iterdir() if d.is_dir()]\n            if dirs:\n                provider_output_dir = dirs[0]\n            else:\n                raise Exception(\n                    f\"Could not find provider output directory for {provider}\"\n                )\n\n        # Upload TTS eval results to S3\n        results_prefix = f\"tts/evals/{run_id}/outputs/{provider}\"\n\n        # Read metrics.json and results.csv before uploading\n        metrics_data = None\n        results_data = None\n\n        metrics_file = provider_output_dir / \"metrics.json\"\n        results_file = provider_output_dir / \"results.csv\"\n\n        if metrics_file.exists():\n            with open(metrics_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                metrics_data = json.load(f)",

        "contents": "\n\n\ndef run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the LLM simulation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running simulation task {task_id} for agent {agent['uuid']} \"\n            f\"with {len(personas)} persona(s), {len(scenarios)} scenario(s), \"\n            f\"and {len(metrics)} metric(s)\"\n        )\n        update_simulation_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        # Create persistent output directory (not deleted after job completes)\n        output_base_dir = Path(\"simulation_outputs\").resolve()\n        output_base_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        temp_path = output_base_dir / task_id\n        temp_path.mkdir(exist_ok=True)",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the LLM simulation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running simulation task {task_id} for agent {agent['uuid']} \"\n            f\"with {len(personas)} persona(s), {len(scenarios)} scenario(s), \"\n            f\"and {len(metrics)} metric(s)\"\n        )\n        update_simulation_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        # Create persistent output directory (not deleted after job completes)\n        output_base_dir = Path(\"simulation_outputs\").resolve()\n        output_base_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=True)\n        temp_path = output_base_dir / task_id\n        temp_path.mkdir(exist_ok=True)",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 844

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n\n\n        ...\n\n\n        try:\n            # Build pense config\n            pense_config = _build_pense_simulation_config(\n                agent, personas, scenarios, metrics\n            )\n            model_to_use = pense_config[\"params\"][\"model\"]\n\n            # Create output directory\n            output_dir = temp_path / \"output\"\n            output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n            # Run pense simulation\n            results_prefix = f\"simulations/runs/{task_id}\"\n            result = _run_pense_simulation(\n                model=model_to_use,\n                pense_config=pense_config,\n                output_dir=output_dir,\n                s3_bucket=s3_bucket,\n                s3_prefix=results_prefix,\n                log_prefix=f\"Simulation {task_id}\",\n            )\n\n            # Update job with results\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\n                    \"total_simulations\": result[\"total_simulations\"],\n                    \"metrics\": result[\"metrics\"],\n                    \"simulation_results\": result[\"simulation_results\"],\n                    \"results_s3_prefix\": results_prefix,\n                    \"error\": result[\"error\"],\n                },\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Simulation task {task_id} completed: \"\n                f\"{result['total_simulations']} simulation(s) run\"\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\"error\": f\"Unexpected error during simulation: {str(e)}\"},\n            )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        update_simulation_job(\n            task_id,\n            status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n            results={\"error\": f\"Task failed: {str(e)}\"},\n        )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "text": "async def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):"

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n        try:\n            # Build pense config\n            pense_config = _build_pense_simulation_config(\n                agent, personas, scenarios, metrics\n            )\n            model_to_use = pense_config[\"params\"][\"model\"]\n\n            # Create output directory\n            output_dir = temp_path / \"output\"\n            output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n            # Run pense simulation\n            results_prefix = f\"simulations/runs/{task_id}\"\n            result = _run_pense_simulation(\n                model=model_to_use,\n                pense_config=pense_config,\n                output_dir=output_dir,\n                s3_bucket=s3_bucket,\n                s3_prefix=results_prefix,\n                log_prefix=f\"Simulation {task_id}\",\n            )\n\n            # Update job with results\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\n                    \"total_simulations\": result[\"total_simulations\"],\n                    \"metrics\": result[\"metrics\"],\n                    \"simulation_results\": result[\"simulation_results\"],\n                    \"results_s3_prefix\": results_prefix,\n                    \"error\": result[\"error\"],\n                },\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Simulation task {task_id} completed: \"\n                f\"{result['total_simulations']} simulation(s) run\"\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\"error\": f\"Unexpected error during simulation: {str(e)}\"},\n            )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        update_simulation_job(\n            task_id,\n            status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n            results={\"error\": f\"Task failed: {str(e)}\"},\n        )",
        "range": {

          }

{
  "codeResults": [
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Run Simulation API ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense simulation config from agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config containing system_prompt and llm.model\n        personas: List of persona dicts with description and config (containing gender, language)\n        scenarios: List of scenario dicts with description\n        metrics: List of metric dicts with name and description (for evaluation_criteria)\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from agent config\n    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Build personas list as objects with label, characteristics, gender, and language\n    persona_list = []\n    for persona in personas:\n        persona_config = persona.get(\"config\") or {}\n        persona_obj = {\n            \"label\": persona.get(\"name\", \"\"),  # Store persona name/label\n            \"characteristics\": persona.get(\"description\") or persona.get(\"name\"),\n            \"gender\": persona_config.get(\"gender\", \"female\"),\n            \"language\": persona_config.get(\"language\", \"english\"),\n        }\n        if persona_obj[\"characteristics\"]:\n            persona_list.append(persona_obj)\n\n    # Build scenarios list as objects with name and description\n    scenario_list = []",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Run Simulation API ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense simulation config from agent, personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config containing system_prompt and llm.model\n        personas: List of persona dicts with description and config (containing gender, language)\n        scenarios: List of scenario dicts with description\n        metrics: List of metric dicts with name and description (for evaluation_criteria)\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from agent config\n    llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n    model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Build personas list as objects with label, characteristics, gender, and language\n    persona_list = []\n    for persona in personas:\n        persona_config = persona.get(\"config\") or {}\n        persona_obj = {\n            \"label\": persona.get(\"name\", \"\"),  #         "originalContents": "      \"assignments\": [],\n      \"tool_call_sound\": null,\n      \"tool_call_sound_behavior\": \"auto\",\n      \"execution_mode\": \"immediate\",\n      \"expects_response\": false,\n      \"response_timeout_secs\": 1,\n      \"parameters\": [\n        {\n          \"id\": \"next_unanswered_question_index\",\n          \"type\": \"integer\",\n          \"value_type\": \"llm_prompt\",\n          \"description\": \"the index of the next unanswered question that should be asked to the user next. don't repeat questions that have been asked before already in the previous responses.\",\n          \"dynamic_variable\": \"\",\n          \"constant_value\": \"\",\n          \"required\": true\n        },\n        {\n          \"id\": \"questions_answered\",\n          \"type\": \"array\",\n          \"description\": \"Optional, the indices of the questions in the full list of questions that have been answered by the user's last response\",\n          \"items\": {\n            \"type\": \"integer\",\n            \"description\": \"the index of each question that got answered by the current response of the user\"\n          },\n          \"required\": true,\n          \"value_type\": \"llm_prompt\"\n        }\n      ]\n    }\n  ], // tools that the voice agent has access to\n  \"personas\": [\n    // array of persona objects for users in the simulation\n    {\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n# ============ Shared Helper Functions ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    tests: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    model: Optional[str] = None,\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense test config from agent and tests.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config\n        tests: List of test dicts with config\n        model: Optional model override. If None, uses agent's llm.model or defaults to gpt-4.1\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from param or agent config\n    if model is None:\n        llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n        model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Combine test cases from all tests\n    all_test_cases = []\n    for test in tests:\n        test_config = test.get(\"config\")\n        if not test_config:\n            continue\n\n        if test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"type\"] == \"tool_call\":\n            tool_calls = []\n            for tool_call in test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"]:\n                tool_calls.append(\n                    {\n                        \"tool\": tool_call[\"tool\"],\n                        \"arguments\": (\n                            tool_call[\"arguments\"]\n                            if not tool_call.get(\"accept_any_arguments\", False)\n                            else None\n                        ),\n                    }\n                )\n            test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"] = tool_calls\n\n        all_test_cases.append(test_config)\n\n    return {\n        \"params\": {\"model\": model},\n        \"system_prompt\": agent_config.get(\"system_prompt\", \"\"),\n        \"tools\": tool_configs,\n        \"test_cases\": all_test_cases,\n    }",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 180,
            "text": "@router.delete(\"\")"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 181,
            "text": "async def delete_agent_test_link(agent_test: AgentTestDelete):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 185
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 186,
            "text": "    return {\"message\": \"Test removed from agent successfully\"}"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 187
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 188
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 189,
            "text": "# ============ Shared Helper Functions ============"
          },

            "lineNumber": 190

            "lineNumber": 191

          {

          },

          },

          },

            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 255,
            "text": "def _run_pense_test("
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 256,
            "text": "    model: str,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 257,
            "text": "    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 258,
            "text": "    output_dir: Path,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 259,
            "text": "    s3_bucket: str,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 260,
            "text": "    s3_prefix: str,"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 261,
            "text": "    log_prefix: str = \"LLM test\","
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 262,
            "text": ") -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 263,
            "text": "    "
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n# ============ Shared Helper Functions ============\n\n\ndef _build_pense_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    tests: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    model: Optional[str] = None,\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Build the pense test config from agent and tests.\n\n    Args:\n        agent: Agent dict with config\n        tests: List of test dicts with config\n        model: Optional model override. If None, uses agent's llm.model or defaults to gpt-4.1\n    \"\"\"\n    agent_config = agent.get(\"config\") or {}\n\n    # Get model from param or agent config\n    if model is None:\n        llm_config = agent_config.get(\"llm\", {})\n        model = llm_config.get(\"model\", \"gpt-4.1\")\n\n    # Get tools from agent_tools table\n    agent_tools = get_tools_for_agent(agent[\"uuid\"])\n    tool_configs = [\n        {\n            \"name\": tool[\"name\"],\n            \"description\": tool[\"description\"],\n            \"parameters\": tool.get(\"config\", {}).get(\"parameters\", []),\n        }\n        for tool in agent_tools\n    ]\n\n    # Combine test cases from all tests\n    all_test_cases = []\n    for test in tests:\n        test_config = test.get(\"config\")\n        if not test_config:\n            continue\n\n        if test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"type\"] == \"tool_call\":\n            tool_calls = []\n            for tool_call in test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"]:\n                tool_calls.append(\n                    {\n                        \"tool\": tool_call[\"tool\"],\n                        \"arguments\": (\n                            tool_call[\"arguments\"]\n                            if not tool_call.get(\"accept_any_arguments\", False)\n                            else None\n                        ),\n                    }\n                )\n            test_config[\"evaluation\"][\"tool_calls\"] = tool_calls\n\n        all_test_cases.append(test_config)\n\n    return {\n        \"params\": {\"model\": model},\n        \"system_prompt\": agent_config.get(\"system_prompt\", \"\"),\n        \"tools\": tool_configs,\n        \"test_cases\": all_test_cases,\n    }",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 387
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 179
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/agent_tests.py"
      },
      "score": 0.4223943054676056
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "      \"name\": \"question_completeness\", // unique name for the criterion\n      \"description\": \"Whether all the questions in the form were covered in the conversation\" // description used by LLM judge to evaluate\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"assistant_behavior\",\n      \"description\": \"Whether the assistant asks one concise question per turn; is empathetic and does not repeat the user's answer back to them.\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nEach simulation pairs every persona with every scenario. The runner fans out into separate folders named &#96;simulation_persona_\u003cn\u003e_scenario_\u003cm\u003e&#96; and saves transcripts, evaluation results, plus logs for inspection. Use the sample persona/scenario set from the example config or replace them with your own scripted behaviors (e.g., \"refuses to share phone number until probed twice\"). Use these artifacts to verify that the agent follows your instructions (e.g., responds appropriately, calls the right tools at the right times, and terminates the call correctly), then iterate on the prompts/config as needed.\n\nAfter each simulation, an LLM judge evaluates the transcript against all &#96;evaluation_criteria&#96; defined in the config. Each criterion produces a match (true/false) and reasoning. The results are aggregated across all simulations at the end of the run.\n\nSet the required environment variables:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# For OpenAI provider\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For OpenRouter provider\nexport OPENROUTER_API_KEY=your_key\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThen launch simulations:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense llm simulations run \\\n  -c /path/to/config.json \\\n  -o /path/to/output \\\n  -m \u003cmodel_name\u003e \\\n  -p \u003cprovider\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 649,
            "text": "    {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 650,
            "text": "      \"name\": \"question_completeness\", // unique name for the criterion"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 651,
            "text": "      \"description\": \"Whether all the questions in the form were covered in the conversation\" // description used by LLM judge to evaluate"

          },

            "lineNumber": 653,
            "text": "    {"
          },

            "lineNumber": 654,
            "text": "      \"name\": \"assistant_behavior\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 655,
            "text": "      \"description\": \"Whether the assistant asks one concise question per turn; is empathetic and does not repeat the user's answer back to them.\""
          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 657,

            "lineNumber": 660

          {
            "lineNumber": 661,
            "text": "Each simulation pairs every persona with every scenario. The runner fans out into separate folders named &#96;simulation_persona_\u003cn\u003e_scenario_\u003cm\u003e&#96; and saves transcripts, evaluation results, plus logs for inspection. Use the sample persona/scenario set from the example config or replace them with your own scripted behaviors (e.g., \"refuses to share phone number until probed twice\"). Use these artifacts to verify that the agent follows your instructions (e.g., responds appropriately, calls the right tools at the right times, and terminates the call correctly), then iterate on the prompts/config as needed."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 662
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 663,
            "text": "After each simulation, an LLM judge evaluates the transcript against all &#96;evaluation_criteria&#96; defined in the config. Each criterion produces a match (true/false) and reasoning. The results are aggregated across all simulations at the end of the run."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 664
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 665,
            "text": "Set the required environment variables:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 666
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 667,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 668,
            "text": "# For OpenAI provider"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 669,
            "text": "export OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 670
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 671,
            "text": "# For OpenRouter provider"
          },

          },

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 674
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 675,
            "text": "Then launch simulations:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 676
          },

            "lineNumber": 677,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 678,
            "text": "pense llm simulations run \\"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 679,
            "text": "  -c /path/to/config.json \\"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 680,

          },

            "lineNumber": 681,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 683,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 684
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 685,
            "text": "**Provider Options:**"
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "      \"name\": \"question_completeness\", // unique name for the criterion\n      \"description\": \"Whether all the questions in the form were covered in the conversation\" // description used by LLM judge to evaluate\n    },\n    {\n      \"name\": \"assistant_behavior\",\n      \"description\": \"Whether the assistant asks one concise question per turn; is empathetic and does not repeat the user's answer back to them.\"\n    }\n  ]\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nEach simulation pairs every persona with every scenario. The runner fans out into separate folders named &#96;simulation_persona_\u003cn\u003e_scenario_\u003cm\u003e&#96; and saves transcripts, evaluation results, plus logs for inspection. Use the sample persona/scenario set from the example config or replace them with your own scripted behaviors (e.g., \"refuses to share phone number until probed twice\"). Use these artifacts to verify that the agent follows your instructions (e.g., responds appropriately, calls the right tools at the right times, and terminates the call correctly), then iterate on the prompts/config as needed.\n\nAfter each simulation, an LLM judge evaluates the transcript against all &#96;evaluation_criteria&#96; defined in the config. Each criterion produces a match (true/false) and reasoning. The results are aggregated across all simulations at the end of the run.\n\nSet the required environment variables:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\n# For OpenAI provider\nexport OPENAI_API_KEY=your_key\n\n# For OpenRouter provider\nexport OPENROUTER_API_KEY=your_key\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nThen launch simulations:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense llm simulations run \\\n  -c /path/to/config.json \\\n  -o /path/to/output \\\n  -m \u003cmodel_name\u003e \\\n  -p \u003cprovider\u003e\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_test(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM test\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm tests run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_tests, passed, failed, test_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file = output_dir / \"test_config.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm tests run command\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"tests\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Find results.json and metrics.json files\n    results_data = None\n    metrics_data = None",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_test(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM test\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm tests run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_tests, passed, failed, test_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file = output_dir / \"test_config.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm tests run command\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"tests\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Find results.json and metrics.json files\n    results_data = None\n    metrics_data = None",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 387
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 191
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/agent_tests.py"
      },

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "2. Pick or edit a config file. &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/config.json&#96; is a ready-to-use config file.\n   You can configure the providers for STT, LLM, and TTS in the config file as shown below:\n\n   **Supported Providers:**\n\n   - **STT**: deepgram, google, openai, elevenlabs, sarvam, cartesia, smallest\n   - **TTS**: elevenlabs, cartesia, google, openai, smallest, deepgram, sarvam\n   - **LLM**: openrouter, openai\n\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;json\n   {\n     \"system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful assistant.\",\n     \"language\": \"english\",\n     \"stt\": {\n       \"provider\": \"deepgram\"\n     },\n     \"tts\": {\n       \"provider\": \"cartesia\",\n       \"voice_id\": \"YOUR_VOICE_ID\"\n     },\n     \"llm\": {\n       \"provider\": \"openrouter\", // or \"openai\"\n       \"model\": \"openai/gpt-4o-2024-11-20\" // or \"gpt-4.1\"\n     },\n     \"tools\": [\n       {\n         \"type\": \"client\",\n         \"name\": \"get_weather\",\n         \"description\": \"Get the current weather\",\n         \"parameters\": [\n           {\n             \"id\": \"location\",\n             \"type\": \"string\",\n             \"description\": \"The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA\",\n             \"required\": true\n           }\n         ]\n       }\n     ]\n   }\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n3. Start the runner:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense agent test -c pense/agent/examples/test/config.json -o ./out/run\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOnce you run it, you can open &#96;http://localhost:7860/client/&#96; in your browser. Click **Connect**, and begin talking to the agent through your browser.\n\n\u003e **⚠️ Important**  \n\u003e Avoid using your laptop's speaker and microphone at the same time. Audio from the speaker can be picked up by the microphone, interrupting the agent with its own voice.  \n\u003e **Use a headphone with a built-in microphone.**\n\nThe client UI streams the conversation transcript in real time, while the **Metrics** tab mirrors the live latency statistics reported by the pipeline as shown in the images below:\n\n![Conversation example](images/run_conversation.png)\n\n![Metrics example](images/run_metrics.png)\n\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 29,
            "text": "1. Make sure your &#96;.env&#96; file has the required API keys (defaults use OpenAI STT + LLM and Google TTS)."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 30,
            "text": "2. Pick or edit a config file. &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/config.json&#96; is a ready-to-use config file."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 31,
            "text": "   You can configure the providers for STT, LLM, and TTS in the config file as shown below:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 32
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 33,
            "text": "   **Supported Providers:**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 34
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 35,
            "text": "   - **STT**: deepgram, google, openai, elevenlabs, sarvam, cartesia, smallest"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "   - **TTS**: elevenlabs, cartesia, google, openai, smallest, deepgram, sarvam"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "   - **LLM**: openrouter, openai"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 38
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 39,
            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;json"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 40,
            "text": "   {"
          },

            "lineNumber": 41,
            "text": "     \"system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful assistant.\","
          },

            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "     \"language\": \"english\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "     \"stt\": {"
          },

          },
          {

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "     \"llm\": {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "       \"provider\": \"openrouter\", // or \"openai\""
          },
          {

            "text": "       \"model\": \"openai/gpt-4o-2024-11-20\" // or \"gpt-4.1\""

          },

            "lineNumber": 54,

            "lineNumber": 55,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 56,
            "text": "         \"type\": \"client\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 57,
            "text": "         \"name\": \"get_weather\","
          },

            "lineNumber": 58,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 59,
            "text": "         \"parameters\": ["
          },

            "lineNumber": 60,
            "text": "           {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 61,
            "text": "             \"id\": \"location\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 62,
            "text": "             \"type\": \"string\","
          },

          },

            "text": "   &#96;&#96;&#96;"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 71
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 72,
            "text": "3. Start the runner:"
          },

          {
            "lineNumber": 74,
            "text": "&#96;&#96;&#96;bash"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 75,
            "text": "pense agent test -c pense/agent/examples/test/config.json -o ./out/run"

            "lineNumber": 76,

          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 77
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 78,
            "text": "Once you run it, you can open &#96;http://localhost:7860/client/&#96; in your browser. Click **Connect**, and begin talking to the agent through your browser."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 79
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 80,
            "text": "\u003e **⚠️ Important**  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 81,
            "text": "\u003e Avoid using your laptop's speaker and microphone at the same time. Audio from the speaker can be picked up by the microphone, interrupting the agent with its own voice.  "
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 82,
            "text": "\u003e **Use a headphone with a built-in microphone.**"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 83
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 84,
            "text": "The client UI streams the conversation transcript in real time, while the **Metrics** tab mirrors the live latency statistics reported by the pipeline as shown in the images below:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 85
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 86,
            "text": "![Conversation example](images/run_conversation.png)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 87
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 88,
            "text": "![Metrics example](images/run_metrics.png)"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 89
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 90,
            "text": "Every user/bot audio turn is persisted inside &#96;\u003coutput_dir\u003e/audios&#96; (see a sample output folder for the exact layout). The terminal also logs each transcript message and every function/tool call issued by the LLM so you can audit the interaction without leaving your shell."
          }

        "originalContents": "2. Pick or edit a config file. &#96;pense/agent/examples/test/config.json&#96; is a ready-to-use config file.\n   You can configure the providers for STT, LLM, and TTS in the config file as shown below:\n\n   **Supported Providers:**\n\n   - **STT**: deepgram, google, openai, elevenlabs, sarvam, cartesia, smallest\n   - **TTS**: elevenlabs, cartesia, google, openai, smallest, deepgram, sarvam\n   - **LLM**: openrouter, openai\n\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;json\n   {\n     \"system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful assistant.\",\n     \"language\": \"english\",\n     \"stt\": {\n       \"provider\": \"deepgram\"\n     },\n     \"tts\": {\n       \"provider\": \"cartesia\",\n       \"voice_id\": \"YOUR_VOICE_ID\"\n     },\n     \"llm\": {\n       \"provider\": \"openrouter\", // or \"openai\"\n       \"model\": \"openai/gpt-4o-2024-11-20\" // or \"gpt-4.1\"\n     },\n     \"tools\": [\n       {\n         \"type\": \"client\",\n         \"name\": \"get_weather\",\n         \"description\": \"Get the current weather\",\n         \"parameters\": [\n           {\n             \"id\": \"location\",\n             \"type\": \"string\",\n             \"description\": \"The city and state, e.g. San Francisco, CA\",\n             \"required\": true\n           }\n         ]\n       }\n     ]\n   }\n   &#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n3. Start the runner:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\npense agent test -c pense/agent/examples/test/config.json -o ./out/run\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nOnce you run it, you can open &#96;http://localhost:7860/client/&#96; in your browser. Click **Connect**, and begin talking to the agent through your browser.\n\n\u003e **⚠️ Important**  \n\u003e Avoid using your laptop's speaker and microphone at the same time. Audio from the speaker can be picked up by the microphone, interrupting the agent with its own voice.  \n\u003e **Use a headphone with a built-in microphone.**\n\nThe client UI streams the conversation transcript in real time, while the **Metrics** tab mirrors the live latency statistics reported by the pipeline as shown in the images below:\n\n![Conversation example](images/run_conversation.png)\n\n![Metrics example](images/run_metrics.png)\n\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def run_simulation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n\n\n        ...\n\n\n        try:\n            # Build pense config\n            pense_config = _build_pense_simulation_config(\n                agent, personas, scenarios, metrics\n            )\n            model_to_use = pense_config[\"params\"][\"model\"]\n\n            # Create output directory\n            output_dir = temp_path / \"output\"\n            output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n            # Run pense simulation\n            results_prefix = f\"simulations/runs/{task_id}\"\n            result = _run_pense_simulation(\n                model=model_to_use,\n                pense_config=pense_config,\n                output_dir=output_dir,\n                s3_bucket=s3_bucket,\n                s3_prefix=results_prefix,\n                log_prefix=f\"Simulation {task_id}\",\n            )\n\n            # Update job with results\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\n                    \"total_simulations\": result[\"total_simulations\"],\n                    \"metrics\": result[\"metrics\"],\n                    \"simulation_results\": result[\"simulation_results\"],\n                    \"results_s3_prefix\": results_prefix,\n                    \"error\": result[\"error\"],\n                },\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Simulation task {task_id} completed: \"\n                f\"{result['total_simulations']} simulation(s) run\"\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\"error\": f\"Unexpected error during simulation: {str(e)}\"},\n            )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        update_simulation_job(\n            task_id,\n            status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n            results={\"error\": f\"Task failed: {str(e)}\"},\n        )",

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n        try:\n            # Build pense config\n            pense_config = _build_pense_simulation_config(\n                agent, personas, scenarios, metrics\n            )\n            model_to_use = pense_config[\"params\"][\"model\"]\n\n            # Create output directory\n            output_dir = temp_path / \"output\"\n            output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n            # Run pense simulation\n            results_prefix = f\"simulations/runs/{task_id}\"\n            result = _run_pense_simulation(\n                model=model_to_use,\n                pense_config=pense_config,\n                output_dir=output_dir,\n                s3_bucket=s3_bucket,\n                s3_prefix=results_prefix,\n                log_prefix=f\"Simulation {task_id}\",\n            )\n\n            # Update job with results\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\n                    \"total_simulations\": result[\"total_simulations\"],\n                    \"metrics\": result[\"metrics\"],\n                    \"simulation_results\": result[\"simulation_results\"],\n                    \"results_s3_prefix\": results_prefix,\n                    \"error\": result[\"error\"],\n                },\n            )\n\n            logger.info(\n                f\"Simulation task {task_id} completed: \"\n                f\"{result['total_simulations']} simulation(s) run\"\n            )\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            traceback.print_exc()\n            update_simulation_job(\n                task_id,\n                status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n                results={\"error\": f\"Unexpected error during simulation: {str(e)}\"},\n            )\n\n    except Exception as e:\n        traceback.print_exc()\n        update_simulation_job(\n            task_id,\n            status=TaskStatus.DONE.value,\n            results={\"error\": f\"Task failed: {str(e)}\"},\n        )",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 921
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "column": 5,
            "line": 764
          }

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def _build_pense_simulation_config(\n    agent: Dict[str, Any],\n    personas: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    scenarios: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n    metrics: List[Dict[str, Any]],\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n\n    ...\n\n    for scenario in scenarios:\n        scenario_obj = {\n            \"name\": scenario.get(\"name\", \"\"),  # Store scenario name/label\n            \"description\": scenario.get(\"description\", \"\"),\n        }\n        scenario_list.append(scenario_obj)\n\n    # Build evaluation criteria from metrics\n    evaluation_criteria = [\n        {\n            \"name\": metric.get(\"name\"),\n            \"description\": metric.get(\"description\") or metric.get(\"name\"),\n        }\n        for metric in metrics\n        if metric.get(\"name\")\n    ]\n\n    return {\n        \"params\": {\"model\": model},\n        \"agent_system_prompt\": agent_config.get(\"system_prompt\", \"\"),\n        \"tools\": tool_configs,\n        \"personas\": persona_list,\n        \"scenarios\": scenario_list,\n        \"evaluation_criteria\": evaluation_criteria,\n    }",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nEach column after &#96;model&#96; represents a test config (scenario), and &#96;overall&#96; is the aggregate pass percentage across all configs.\n\nYou can checkout [&#96;pense/llm/examples/tests/leaderboard&#96;](pense/llm/examples/tests/leaderboard) to see a sample output of the leaderboard script.\n\n![Leaderboard example](pense/llm/examples/tests/leaderboard/llm_leaderboard.png)\n\n## LLM simulations\n\nRun fully automated, text-only conversations between two LLMs—the “agent” plus multiple users having specific personas to mimic specific scenarios given by you. Prepare a JSON configuration file as shown below:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"instructions for the bot\",\n  \"tools\": [...], // tool schemas available to the agent LLM\n  \"personas\": [\n    // array of persona objects for users in the simulation\n    {\n      \"characteristics\": \"description of user personality, background, and behavior\",\n      \"gender\": \"female\", // gender of the persona (used in user system prompt)\n      \"language\": \"english\" // language for both agent and user in the simulation\n    }\n  ],\n  \"scenarios\": [\n    // array of scenario objects to be run with each user persona\n    {\n      \"description\": \"description of the scenario to be played out\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"evaluation_criteria\": [\n    // array of criteria to evaluate each simulation against\n    {\n",
        "detailedLines": [

            "lineNumber": 616,

            "lineNumber": 618
          },

            "lineNumber": 619,

        "originalContents": "&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nEach column after &#96;model&#96; represents a test config (scenario), and &#96;overall&#96; is the aggregate pass percentage across all configs.\n\nYou can checkout [&#96;pense/llm/examples/tests/leaderboard&#96;](pense/llm/examples/tests/leaderboard) to see a sample output of the leaderboard script.\n\n![Leaderboard example](pense/llm/examples/tests/leaderboard/llm_leaderboard.png)\n\n## LLM simulations\n\nRun fully automated, text-only conversations between two LLMs—the “agent” plus multiple users having specific personas to mimic specific scenarios given by you. Prepare a JSON configuration file as shown below:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"instructions for the bot\",\n  \"tools\": [...], // tool schemas available to the agent LLM\n  \"personas\": [\n    // array of persona objects for users in the simulation\n    {\n      \"characteristics\": \"description of user personality, background, and behavior\",\n      \"gender\": \"female\", // gender of the persona (used in user system prompt)\n      \"language\": \"english\" // language for both agent and user in the simulation\n    }\n  ],\n  \"scenarios\": [\n    // array of scenario objects to be run with each user persona\n    {\n      \"description\": \"description of the scenario to be played out\"\n    }\n  ],\n  \"evaluation_criteria\": [\n    // array of criteria to evaluate each simulation against\n    {\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{simulation_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a simulation with personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM simulations command\n    with the agent's config and the simulation's personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Uses the agent linked to the simulation and its LLM model configuration.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Simulation not found\")\n\n    # Get agent from simulation\n    agent_uuid = simulation.get(\"agent_id\")\n    if not agent_uuid:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"No agent linked to this simulation. Link an agent to the simulation first.\",\n        )\n\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Get linked entities\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no personas. Add at least one persona.\",\n        )\n\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no scenarios. Add at least one scenario.\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,
