          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{simulation_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a simulation with personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM simulations command\n    with the agent's config and the simulation's personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Uses the agent linked to the simulation and its LLM model configuration.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Simulation not found\")\n\n    # Get agent from simulation\n    agent_uuid = simulation.get(\"agent_id\")\n    if not agent_uuid:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"No agent linked to this simulation. Link an agent to the simulation first.\",\n        )\n\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Get linked entities\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no personas. Add at least one persona.\",\n        )\n\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no scenarios. Add at least one scenario.\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "line": 921
          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 767
          }
        },
        "relativeWorkspacePath": "src/routers/simulations.py"
      },
      "score": 0.3678840398788452
    },
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "# Pense\n\nAn open-source simulation and testing framework for voice agents.\n\nWith Pense, you can move from a slow, manual testing to a fast, automated, and repeatable testing process:\n\n- Evaluate different components of your voice agents across multiple vendors in isolation\n- Create comprehensive test suites that verify both conversational responses and tool usage\n- Simulate entire conversations spanning thousands of scenarios across multiple user personas\n\nPense is built on top of [pipecat](https://github.com/pipecat-ai/pipecat), a framework for building voice agents.\n\n## Setup\n\nWe use &#96;uv&#96; to manage the dependencies. Make sure you have it installed on your system.\n\nCreate the virtual environment and install the dependencies.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nuv sync --frozen\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nCopy &#96;.env.example&#96; to &#96;.env&#96; and fill in the API keys for the providers you want to evaluate. All modules (agent, stt, tts, llm) will use this single &#96;.env&#96; file:\n\n## Talk to the agent\n\n&#96;pense/agent/test.py&#96; spins up the fully interactive STT → LLM → TTS pipeline so you can speak with the agent in real time.\n\n1. Make sure your &#96;.env&#96; file has the required API keys (defaults use OpenAI STT + LLM and Google TTS).\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

            "lineNumber": 2
          },

            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "An open-source simulation and testing framework for voice agents."

            "lineNumber": 4
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "With Pense, you can move from a slow, manual testing to a fast, automated, and repeatable testing process:"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "- Evaluate different components of your voice agents across multiple vendors in isolation"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "- Create comprehensive test suites that verify both conversational responses and tool usage"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 9,
            "text": "- Simulate entire conversations spanning thousands of scenarios across multiple user personas"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 10
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 11,
            "text": "Pense is built on top of [pipecat](https://github.com/pipecat-ai/pipecat), a framework for building voice agents."
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 12
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 13,
            "text": "## Setup"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 14
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 15,
            "text": "We use &#96;uv&#96; to manage the dependencies. Make sure you have it installed on your system."
          },

            "lineNumber": 16
          },

            "lineNumber": 21,

          },

            "lineNumber": 22
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 23,
            "text": "Copy &#96;.env.example&#96; to &#96;.env&#96; and fill in the API keys for the providers you want to evaluate. All modules (agent, stt, tts, llm) will use this single &#96;.env&#96; file:"
          },

        "originalContents": "# Pense\n\nAn open-source simulation and testing framework for voice agents.\n\nWith Pense, you can move from a slow, manual testing to a fast, automated, and repeatable testing process:\n\n- Evaluate different components of your voice agents across multiple vendors in isolation\n- Create comprehensive test suites that verify both conversational responses and tool usage\n- Simulate entire conversations spanning thousands of scenarios across multiple user personas\n\nPense is built on top of [pipecat](https://github.com/pipecat-ai/pipecat), a framework for building voice agents.\n\n## Setup\n\nWe use &#96;uv&#96; to manage the dependencies. Make sure you have it installed on your system.\n\nCreate the virtual environment and install the dependencies.\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;bash\nuv sync --frozen\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nCopy &#96;.env.example&#96; to &#96;.env&#96; and fill in the API keys for the providers you want to evaluate. All modules (agent, stt, tts, llm) will use this single &#96;.env&#96; file:\n\n## Talk to the agent\n\n&#96;pense/agent/test.py&#96; spins up the fully interactive STT → LLM → TTS pipeline so you can speak with the agent in real time.\n\n1. Make sure your &#96;.env&#96; file has the required API keys (defaults use OpenAI STT + LLM and Google TTS).\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "│   └── results.log              # terminal output of the simulation\n├── simulation_persona_1_scenario_2\n│   └── ...\n├── results.csv                  # aggregated results for all simulations\n└── metrics.json                 # summary statistics for each criterion\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;config.json&#96; in each simulation directory contains the persona and scenario used for that specific simulation:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"persona\": {\n    \"characteristics\": \"description of user personality, background, and behavior\",\n    \"gender\": \"female\",\n    \"language\": \"english\"\n  },\n  \"scenario\": {\n    \"description\": \"the scenario description for this simulation\"\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;evaluation_results.csv&#96; contains the evaluation results for each criterion in a simulation:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csv\nname,match,reasoning\nquestion_completeness,True,\"The assistant asked for the user's full name, address, and telephone number...\"\nassistant_behavior,True,\"The assistant asked one concise question per turn...\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;results.csv&#96; aggregates the match scores across all simulations:\n",

        "originalContents": "│   └── results.log              # terminal output of the simulation\n├── simulation_persona_1_scenario_2\n│   └── ...\n├── results.csv                  # aggregated results for all simulations\n└── metrics.json                 # summary statistics for each criterion\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;config.json&#96; in each simulation directory contains the persona and scenario used for that specific simulation:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"persona\": {\n    \"characteristics\": \"description of user personality, background, and behavior\",\n    \"gender\": \"female\",\n    \"language\": \"english\"\n  },\n  \"scenario\": {\n    \"description\": \"the scenario description for this simulation\"\n  }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;evaluation_results.csv&#96; contains the evaluation results for each criterion in a simulation:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;csv\nname,match,reasoning\nquestion_completeness,True,\"The assistant asked for the user's full name, address, and telephone number...\"\nassistant_behavior,True,\"The assistant asked one concise question per turn...\"\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n&#96;results.csv&#96; aggregates the match scores across all simulations:\n",

      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"params\": {\n    \"model\": \"gpt-4.1\" // the name of the openai model\n  },\n  \"system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful assistant...\", // the system prompt for the LLM.\n  \"tools\": [\n    // array of tools that the LLM can call. Each tool should have:\n    {\n      \"type\": \"client\", // the type of the tool. Currently only \"client\" is supported\n      \"name\": \"plan_next_question\", // the name of the tool\n      \"description\": \"Tool description...\", // the description of the tool\n      \"parameters\": [\n        // array of parameters that the tool accepts\n        {\n          \"id\": \"next_unanswered_question_index\", // the id of the parameter\n          \"type\": \"integer\", // the type of the parameter\n          \"description\": \"Parameter description...\", // the description of the parameter\n          \"required\": true // whether the parameter is required\n        }\n      ]\n    }\n  ],\n  \"test_cases\": [\n    // array of test cases, each containing:\n",

        "originalContents": "&#96;&#96;&#96;json\n{\n  \"params\": {\n    \"model\": \"gpt-4.1\" // the name of the openai model\n  },\n  \"system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful assistant...\", // the system prompt for the LLM.\n  \"tools\": [\n    // array of tools that the LLM can call. Each tool should have:\n    {\n      \"type\": \"client\", // the type of the tool. Currently only \"client\" is supported\n      \"name\": \"plan_next_question\", // the name of the tool\n      \"description\": \"Tool description...\", // the description of the tool\n      \"parameters\": [\n        // array of parameters that the tool accepts\n        {\n          \"id\": \"next_unanswered_question_index\", // the id of the parameter\n          \"type\": \"integer\", // the type of the parameter\n          \"description\": \"Parameter description...\", // the description of the parameter\n          \"required\": true // whether the parameter is required\n        }\n      ]\n    }\n  ],\n  \"test_cases\": [\n    // array of test cases, each containing:\n",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "{\n  \"params\": {\n    \"model\": \"google/gemini-3-flash-preview\"\n  },\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n4: Age (number)\\n5: Previous stillbirth or neonatal loss?\\n6: History of 3 or more consecutive spontaneous abortions\\n7: Birth weight of last baby (string)\\n8: Last pregnancy: hospital admission for hypertension or pre-eclampsia/eclampsia?\\n9: Previous surgery on reproductive tract (Caesarean section, myomectomy, cone biopsy, cervical cerclage,)\\n10: Diagnosed or suspected multiple pregnancy\\n11: Isoimmunisation Rh (-) in current or previous pregnancy\\n12: Vaginal bleeding\\n13: Pelvic mass\\n14: Diastolic blood pressure 90 mmHg or more at booking\\n15: Diabetes mellitus on insulin or oral hypoglycaemic treatment\\n16: Cardiac disease\\n17: Renal disease\\n18: Epilepsy\\n19: Asthmatic on medication\\n20: Tuberculosis\\n21: Known \\u2018substance\\u2019 abuse (including heavy alcohol drinking)\\n22: Any other severe medical disease or condition (string)\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience, if they have given this answer across multiple messages consider that valid too - don't force the user to repeat the number in one go if all the 10 digits are already clear from the conversation)\\nAge: must be a valid age value\\nBirth weight of last baby: must be a valid weight value with the appropriate units (except if this is their first baby, in which case this should be null\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nThe user may have given the answer to a question in parts across several messages. For example, they might have clarified something in a later message or corrected something they missed earlier. So, make sure to look for the complete answer in the user's response across all the messages and don't unnecessarily repeat the same question to the user as long as the full answer is clear from the entire conversation history so far.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive True/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\nWhenever you invoke the &#96;fill_form&#96; tool, always use Roman characters for the string values\",\n",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 1,
            "text": "{"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 2,
            "text": "  \"params\": {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 3,
            "text": "    \"model\": \"google/gemini-3-flash-preview\""
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 4,
            "text": "  },"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 5,
            "text": "  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n4: Age (number)\\n5: Previous stillbirth or neonatal loss?\\n6: History of 3 or more consecutive spontaneous abortions\\n7: Birth weight of last baby (string)\\n8: Last pregnancy: hospital admission for hypertension or pre-eclampsia/eclampsia?\\n9: Previous surgery on reproductive tract (Caesarean section, myomectomy, cone biopsy, cervical cerclage,)\\n10: Diagnosed or suspected multiple pregnancy\\n11: Isoimmunisation Rh (-) in current or previous pregnancy\\n12: Vaginal bleeding\\n13: Pelvic mass\\n14: Diastolic blood pressure 90 mmHg or more at booking\\n15: Diabetes mellitus on insulin or oral hypoglycaemic treatment\\n16: Cardiac disease\\n17: Renal disease\\n18: Epilepsy\\n19: Asthmatic on medication\\n20: Tuberculosis\\n21: Known \\u2018substance\\u2019 abuse (including heavy alcohol drinking)\\n22: Any other severe medical disease or condition (string)\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Ask the questions sequentially, one at a time. Make sure to always speak out the next question for the user. They don't know what the next question will be. It is your responsibility to ask them the next question.\\n\\nDo not repeat the user's answer back to them.\\n\\nExcept for the questions where a type has been explicitly given beside it, the rest of the questions are boolean questions (yes/no).\\n\\nIf the user gives an answer that is not valid for a question, then, don't call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool at all.\\n\\nOnly if the user gives a valid answer to a question, then, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool.\\n\\n# Important instructions\\nFor each field, you must make sure that you get valid and complete answers from the user.\\n\\nName: the full name including both the first name and last name (users might have initials as part of their name - don't ask them to expand it)\\nAddress: the full address of the user's specific place of residence\\nTelephone: must be a valid 10-digit phone number (don't bug the user to say the number in 10 digits or without spaces or without hyphens or to repeat it as long you can infer the number from your message, irrespective of whatever format it may have been given in - asking for repeating information that is already clear is not a good user experience, if they have given this answer across multiple messages consider that valid too - don't force the user to repeat the number in one go if all the 10 digits are already clear from the conversation)\\nAge: must be a valid age value\\nBirth weight of last baby: must be a valid weight value with the appropriate units (except if this is their first baby, in which case this should be null\\n\\nUntil you get a valid and complete response for a specific question from the user, keep probing for more details until you have extracted all the details required to meet the criteria of completeness for that question.\\n\\nThe user may have given the answer to a question in parts across several messages. For example, they might have clarified something in a later message or corrected something they missed earlier. So, make sure to look for the complete answer in the user's response across all the messages and don't unnecessarily repeat the same question to the user as long as the full answer is clear from the entire conversation history so far.\\n\\nFor the boolean questions, just look for whether the user has that symptom or not. If the user is not able to give a definitive answer to any the boolean questions, try probing once to help them arrive at a definitive answer. If they are still not able to give a definitive True/false answer, mark that response as null.\\n\\n# Skipping already answered questions\\nIt is possible that even though you have asked a particular question, the user's response may end up answering some of the future questions you were going to ask. If the user's response already definitively answers those questions, then, don't repeat those questions again and skip them to move to the next unanswered question.\\n\\nAfter every valid and complete response given by the user to a question, call the &#96;plan_next_question&#96; tool with the indices of the questions in the list of questions above that the user's response has already answered and the index of the next unanswered question that you are going to ask.\\n\\nIf the user's response only answers the exact question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_that_question\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers more questions beyond the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_first_question_answered\u003e, \u003cindex_of_the_second_question_answered\u003e, ....]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the next unanswered question based on the conversation history so far.\\n\\nIf the user's response answers some other question from the form but not the question you asked, then, call &#96;plan_next_question&#96; with &#96;questions_answered&#96; as &#96;[\u003cindex_of_the_question_answered\u003e]&#96; and &#96;next_unanswered_question_index&#96; as the index of the question you had originally asked but the user did not answer.\\n\\nIf the user's response is completely irrelevant to any of the questions in the form, don't call this tool and steer the user back to the conversation.\\n\\nUse the same index values as mentioned in the list above: from 1-22. So, 1-indexed. Not, 0-indexed.\\n\\n# Ensuring completion of all questions\\n- If the user insists on skipping any question when you asked it, make sure to ask it again later. Before ending the call, make sure to ask all the questions that you asked.\\n\\n# Style\\n- Keep the conversation clear, warm, friendly and empathetic. It should feel like a natural and realistic conversation between a nurse and a mother who is pregnant. Make sure each question that you ask is concise and to the point.\\n- Speak in an empathetic, friendly tone like a nurse at a government hospital.\\n- Always stay gender neutral and don't say anything that might indicate any gender or name for you or anthropomorphise you in any way.\\nWhenever you invoke the &#96;fill_form&#96; tool, always use Roman characters for the string values\","
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 6,
            "text": "  \"tools\": ["
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 7,
            "text": "    {"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 8,
            "text": "      \"name\": \"plan_next_question\","
          }
        ],
        "originalContents": "{\n  \"params\": {\n    \"model\": \"google/gemini-3-flash-preview\"\n  },\n  \"agent_system_prompt\": \"You are a helpful nurse speaking to a pregnant mother who has come for an Antenatal visit (ANC visit).\\n\\nYou are helping her with filling the ANC visit form which has the following questions:\\n\\n1: Name of patient (string)\\n2: Address (string)\\n3: Telephone (number)\\n4: Age (number)\\n5: Previous stillbirth or neonatal loss?\\n6: History of 3 or more consecutive spontaneous abortions\\n7: Birth weight of last baby (string)\\n8: Last pregnancy: hospital admission for hypertension or pre-eclampsia/eclampsia?\\n9: Previous surgery on reproductive tract (Caesarean section, myomectomy, cone biopsy, cervical cerclage,)\\n10: Diagnosed or suspected multiple pregnancy\\n11: Isoimmunisation Rh (-) in current or previous pregnancy\\n12: Vaginal bleeding\\n13: Pelvic mass\\n14: Diastolic blood pressure 90 mmHg or more at booking\\n15: Diabetes mellitus on insulin or oral hypoglycaemic treatment\\n16: Cardiac disease\\n17: Renal disease\\n18: Epilepsy\\n19: Asthmatic on medication\\n20: Tuberculosis\\n21: Known \\u2018substance\\u2019 abuse (including heavy alcohol drinking)\\n22: Any other severe medical disease or condition (string)\\n\\nYour goal is to get the answer for all these questions from the user. Lines added: 197, lines removed: 0

```diff
@@ -779,4 +779,201 @@
      }
  
+ 
+ def _run_pense_voice_simulation(
+     pense_config: Dict[str, Any],
+     output_dir: Path,
+     s3_bucket: str,
+     s3_prefix: str,
+     log_prefix: str = "Voice simulation",
+ ) -&gt; Dict[str, Any]:
+     """
+     Run pense agent simulation command and return parsed results.
+ 
+     Args:
+         pense_config: The pense config dict (for voice simulations)
+         output_dir: Directory to write output files
+         s3_bucket: S3 bucket name
+         s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results
+         log_prefix: Prefix for log messages
+ 
+     Returns:
+         Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error, audios_s3_path
+     """
+     s3 = get_s3_client()
+ 
+     # Resolve output directory to absolute path
+     output_dir = output_dir.resolve()
+ 
+     # Create output directory
+     output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+ 
+     # Write config to temp file
+     config_file_name = "simulation_config"
+     config_file = output_dir / f"{config_file_name}.json"
+     with open(config_file, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+         json.dump(pense_config, f, indent=2)
+ 
+     # Run pense agent simulation command
+     # Use absolute paths for config and output
+     run_cmd = [
+         "pense",
+         "agent",
+         "simulation",
+         "-c",
+         str(config_file),
+         "-o",
+         str(output_dir),
+     ]
+ 
+     logger.info(f"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}")
+ 
+     result = subprocess.run(
+         run_cmd,
+         capture_output=True,
+         text=True,
+         cwd=str(output_dir),
+     )
+ 
+     if result.stdout:
+         logger.info(f"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}")
+     if result.stderr:
+         logger.info(f"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}")
+ 
+     # Parse results
+     metrics_data = None
+     results_data = None
+     simulation_results = []
+ 
+     # Find metrics.json and results.csv files
+     metrics_file = output_dir / "metrics.json"
+     results_file = output_dir / "results.csv"
+ 
+     if metrics_file.exists():
+         with open(metrics_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+             metrics_data = json.load(f)
+ 
+     # Parse results.csv for aggregated scores
+     if results_file.exists():
+         import csv
+ 
+         with open(results_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+             reader = csv.DictReader(f)
+             results_data = list(reader)
+ 
+     # Find simulation directories and parse their results
+     # Search recursively for simulation directories (they might be nested)
+     audios_s3_paths = {}
+     for root, dirs, files in os.walk(output_dir):
+         for dir_name in dirs:
+             if dir_name.startswith("simulation_persona_"):
+                 sim_dir = Path(root) / dir_name
+                 sim_name = dir_name
+                 eval_results_file = sim_dir / "evaluation_results.csv"
+                 transcript_file = sim_dir / "transcript.json"
+                 config_file = sim_dir / "config.json"
+                 audios_dir = sim_dir / "audios"
+ 
+                 eval_results = []
+                 if eval_results_file.exists():
+                     import csv
+ 
+                     with open(eval_results_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+                         reader = csv.DictReader(f)
+                         for row in reader:
+                             eval_results.append(
+                                 {
+                                     "name": row.get("name"),
+                                     "match": row.get("match", "").lower() == "true",
+                                     "reasoning": row.get("reasoning", ""),
+                                 }
+                             )
+ 
+                 # Parse transcript.json if it exists
+                 transcript = None
+                 if transcript_file.exists():
+                     try:
+                         with open(transcript_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+                             transcript = json.load(f)
+                     except Exception as e:
+                         logger.warning(
+                             f"Failed to parse transcript.json for {sim_name}: {e}"
+                         )
+ 
+                 # Parse config.json to get persona and scenario data
+                 persona_data = None
+                 scenario_data = None
+                 if config_file.exists():
+                     try:
+                         with open(config_file, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
+                             config_data = json.load(f)
+                             persona_data = config_data.get("persona")
+                             scenario_data = config_data.get("scenario")
+                     except Exception as e:
+                         logger.warning(
+                             f"Failed to parse config.json for {sim_name}: {e}"
+                         )
+ 
+                 # Upload audios folder for this simulation to S3
+                 audios_s3_prefix = None
+                 if audios_dir.exists() and audios_dir.is_dir():
+                     audios_s3_prefix = f"{s3_prefix}/{sim_name}/audios"
+                     audio_files_uploaded = 0
+                     for audio_file in audios_dir.iterdir():
+                         if audio_file.is_file() and (
+                             audio_file.suffix == ".wav"
+                             or audio_file.suffix == ".mp3"
+                             or audio_file.suffix == ".ogg"
+                         ):
+                             relative_audio_path = audio_file.relative_to(output_dir)
+                             audio_s3_key = f"{s3_prefix}/{relative_audio_path}"
+                             s3.upload_file(str(audio_file), s3_bucket, audio_s3_key)
+                             audio_files_uploaded += 1
+                             logger.info(
+                                 f"Uploaded audio file {audio_file.name} to S3: {audio_s3_key}"
+                             )
+                     if audio_files_uploaded &gt; 0:
+                         audios_s3_paths[sim_name] = f"s3://{s3_bucket}/{audios_s3_prefix}"
+                         logger.info(
+                             f"Uploaded {audio_files_uploaded} audio file(s) for {sim_name} to {audios_s3_paths[sim_name]}"
+                         )
+ 
+                 simulation_results.append(
+                     {
+                         "simulation_name": sim_name,
+                         "persona": persona_data,
+                         "scenario": scenario_data,
+                         "evaluation_results": eval_results,
+                         "transcript": transcript,
+                         "audios_s3_path": audios_s3_paths.get(sim_name),
+                     }
+                 )
+ 
+     # Upload all other results to S3 (excluding audios which are already uploaded)
+     for root, dirs, files in os.walk(output_dir):
+         # Skip audios directories as they're already uploaded
+         if "audios" in root:
+             continue
+         for file in files:
+             local_file_path = Path(root) / file
+             relative_path = local_file_path.relative_to(output_dir)
+             s3_key = f"{s3_prefix}/{relative_path}"
+             s3.upload_file(str(local_file_path), s3_bucket, s3_key)
+ 
+     error = None
+     if result.returncode != 0:
+         error = f"Command failed: {result.stderr}"
+ 
+     # Get the base S3 path for audios (directory where all audios are uploaded)
+     base_audios_s3_path = f"s3://{s3_bucket}/{s3_prefix}" if audios_s3_paths else None
+ 
+     return {
+         "success": result.returncode == 0,
+         "total_simulations": len(simulation_results),
+         "metrics": metrics_data,
+         "simulation_results": simulation_results,
+         "error": error,
+         "audios_s3_path": base_audios_s3_path,
+     }
+ 
  
  def run_simulation_task(
```

_**Agent (composer-1)**_

## Summary

1. **Added voice simulation support**: Created `_run_pense_voice_simulation()` that runs `pense agent simulation` instead of `pense llm simulations run` for voice simulations.

2. **Updated config building**: Modified `_build_pense_simulation_config()` to support both chat and voice types:
   - Voice: uses `agent_system_prompt` and includes `interruption_sensitivity` in personas
   - Chat: uses `system_prompt` and includes `params` with model

3. **S3 audio upload**: For voice simulations, uploads all audio files (`.wav`, `.mp3`, `.ogg`) from each simulation's `audios` folder to S3.

4. **Return S3 path**: Returns the S3 path where audios are uploaded:
   - `audios_s3_path` in the results dictionary
   - Added to `SimulationRunStatusResponse` model
   - Format: `s3://{bucket}/{prefix}` pointing to the directory containing all audios

5. **Updated task routing**: Modified `run_simulation_task()` to accept `simulation_type` and route to the appropriate function based on type.

6. **Updated job recovery**: Updated the recovery function to pass `simulation_type` when recovering jobs.

The implementation handles:
- Uploading all audio files from each simulation's `audios` folder
- Avoiding duplicate uploads
- Returning the S3 directory path where audios are stored
- Properly parsing results for both chat and voice simulations

All changes are complete and pass linting.

---

_**Agent (composer-1)**_


## Changes Made

1. Removed top-level `audios_s3_path`: Removed from the return value of `_run_pense_voice_simulation()` and from `results_dict` in `run_simulation_task()`.

2. Removed from response model: Removed `audios_s3_path` from `SimulationRunStatusResponse` since it's no longer at the top level.

3. Added to simulation result model: Added `audios_s3_path` to `SimulationCaseResult` so each simulation result includes its own S3 path.

4. Updated endpoint: Removed `audios_s3_path` from the `get_simulation_run_status` endpoint response.

Now, for voice simulations:
- Each simulation in `simulation_results` includes its own `audios_s3_path` pointing to that simulation's audios folder
- The top-level response no longer includes a single `audios_s3_path`

The `audios_s3_path` is already being set for each simulation result in the code (line 954), so each simulation folder will have its own S3 path returned in the results.

---

_**User (2026-01-15 01:14:24Z)**_

there is an s3 audio prefix for each evaluation result for voice simulation -
when the status for that simulation run is returned, then, if s3 audio prefix  is present, iterate through all the audios in the s3 folder in the order they are present in and return a list of audio paths for each simulation with presigned urls for fetching those audios 

---

{
  "codeResults": [
    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@app.post(\"/presigned-url\", response_model=PresignedURLResponse)\nasync def get_presigned_url(request: PresignedURLRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Generate a presigned URL for uploading files to S3.\n\n    The file will be stored at: bucket/task_type/media/UUID.extension\n\n    Args:\n        request: Contains task_type (stt, tts, agent) and file_extension\n\n    Returns:\n        Presigned URL, S3 path, and expiration time\n    \"\"\"\n    # Validate file extension (remove leading dot if present)\n    file_extension = request.extension\n    if not file_extension:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"File extension cannot be empty\",\n        )\n\n    # Validate task type\n    if request.task_type not in [\"stt\", \"tts\", \"agent\"]:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"task_type must be one of: stt, tts, agent\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 bucket from environment\n    s3_bucket = os.getenv(\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET\")\n    if not s3_bucket:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=500,\n            detail=\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET environment variable is required\",\n        )\n\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Generate UUID for unique file name\n    file_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Construct S3 key: task_type/media/UUID.extension (no prefix)\n    s3_key = f\"{request.task_type}/media/{file_uuid}.{file_extension}\"\n\n    # Generate presigned URL (expires in 1 hour)\n    expiration = 3600  # 1 hour in seconds\n\n    presigned_url = s3.generate_presigned_url(\n        \"put_object\",\n        Params={\n            \"Bucket\": s3_bucket,\n            \"Key\": s3_key,\n            \"ContentType\": request.content_type,\n        },\n        ExpiresIn=expiration,\n    )\n\n    return PresignedURLResponse(\n        presigned_url=presigned_url,\n        s3_path=f\"s3://{s3_bucket}/{s3_key}\",\n        expires_in=expiration,\n    )",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@app.post(\"/presigned-url\", response_model=PresignedURLResponse)\nasync def get_presigned_url(request: PresignedURLRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Generate a presigned URL for uploading files to S3.\n\n    The file will be stored at: bucket/task_type/media/UUID.extension\n\n    Args:\n        request: Contains task_type (stt, tts, agent) and file_extension\n\n    Returns:\n        Presigned URL, S3 path, and expiration time\n    \"\"\"\n    # Validate file extension (remove leading dot if present)\n    file_extension = request.extension\n    if not file_extension:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"File extension cannot be empty\",\n        )\n\n    # Validate task type\n    if request.task_type not in [\"stt\", \"tts\", \"agent\"]:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"task_type must be one of: stt, tts, agent\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 bucket from environment\n    s3_bucket = os.getenv(\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET\")\n    if not s3_bucket:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=500,\n            detail=\"S3_OUTPUT_BUCKET environment variable is required\",\n        )\n\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Generate UUID for unique file name\n    file_uuid = str(uuid.uuid4())\n\n    # Construct S3 key: task_type/media/UUID.extension (no prefix)\n    s3_key = f\"{request.task_type}/media/{file_uuid}.{file_extension}\"\n\n    # Generate presigned URL (expires in 1 hour)\n    expiration = 3600  # 1 hour in seconds\n\n    presigned_url = s3.generate_presigned_url(\n        \"put_object\",\n        Params={\n            \"Bucket\": s3_bucket,\n            \"Key\": s3_key,\n            \"ContentType\": request.content_type,\n        },\n        ExpiresIn=expiration,\n    )\n\n    return PresignedURLResponse(\n        presigned_url=presigned_url,\n        s3_path=f\"s3://{s3_bucket}/{s3_key}\",\n        expires_in=expiration,\n    )",
        "range": {
          "endPosition": {
            "column": 5,
            "line": 149
          },
          "startPosition": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "def evaluate_tts_provider(\n    run_id: str,\n    provider: str,\n    language: str,\n    input_csv: Path,\n    output_dir: Path,\n    port: int,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n) -\u003e ProviderResult:\n\n\n        ...\n\n\n        # Replace local audio paths with presigned S3 URLs in results\n        if results_data:\n            expiration = 3600  # 1 hour expiration\n            for result_row in results_data:\n                if \"audio_path\" in result_row and result_row[\"audio_path\"]:\n                    local_audio_path = result_row[\"audio_path\"]\n                    # Look up S3 key from the mapping\n                    audio_s3_key = audio_path_to_s3_key.get(local_audio_path)\n\n                    if not audio_s3_key:\n                        logger.warning(\n                            f\"Could not find S3 key for audio path: {local_audio_path}\"\n                        )\n                        continue\n\n                    # Generate presigned URL\n                    try:\n                        presigned_url = s3.generate_presigned_url(\n                            \"get_object\",\n                            Params={\n                                \"Bucket\": s3_bucket,\n                                \"Key\": audio_s3_key,\n                            },\n                            ExpiresIn=expiration,\n                        )\n                        result_row[\"audio_path\"] = presigned_url\n                        logger.info(\n                            f\"Replaced audio path with presigned URL for {audio_s3_key}\"\n                        )\n                    except Exception as e:\n                        logger.warning(\n                            f\"Failed to generate presigned URL for {audio_s3_key}: {str(e)}\"\n                        )\n                        # Fallback to S3 path if presigned URL generation fails\n                        result_row[\"audio_path\"] = f\"s3://{s3_bucket}/{audio_s3_key}\"\n\n        return ProviderResult(\n            provider=provider,\n            success=True,\n            message=f\"TTS evaluation completed successfully for {provider}\",\n            metrics=metrics_data,\n            results=results_data,\n        )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

          },

Fallback to S3 path if presigned URL generation fails\n                        result_row[\"audio_path\"] = f\"s3://{s3_bucket}/{audio_s3_key}\"\n\n        return ProviderResult(\n            provider=provider,\n            success=True,\n            message=f\"TTS evaluation completed successfully for {provider}\",\n            metrics=metrics_data,\n            results=results_data,\n        )",

        "contents": "def run_evaluation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    request: STTEvaluationRequest,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n\n                ...\n\n                with open(stt_csv_path, \"w\", newline=\"\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as csvfile:\n                    writer = csv.writer(csvfile)\n                    writer.writerow([\"id\", \"text\"])\n\n                    for idx, (audio_path, gt_text) in enumerate(\n                        zip(request.audio_paths, request.texts)\n                    ):\n                        # Parse S3 path (format: s3://bucket/key or bucket/key)\n                        if audio_path.startswith(\"s3://\"):\n                            parts = audio_path[5:].split(\"/\", 1)\n                            bucket = parts[0]\n                            key = parts[1] if len(parts) \u003e 1 else \"\"\n                        else:\n                            parts = audio_path.split(\"/\", 1)\n                            bucket = parts[0]\n                            key = parts[1] if len(parts) \u003e 1 else \"\"\n\n                        # Generate audio ID\n                        audio_id = f\"audio_{idx + 1}\"\n\n                        # Download audio file\n                        local_wav_path = audios_wav_dir / f\"{audio_id}.wav\"\n                        local_pcm16_path = audios_pcm16_dir / f\"{audio_id}.wav\"\n\n                        logger.info(\n                            f\"Downloading audio file from {bucket}/{key} to {local_wav_path}\"\n                        )\n                        s3.download_file(bucket, key, str(local_wav_path))\n\n                        #       "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef get_s3_client():\n    \"\"\"Get S3 client from environment variables.\"\"\"\n    aws_access_key_id = os.getenv(\"AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID\")\n    aws_secret_access_key = os.getenv(\"AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY\")\n    aws_region = os.getenv(\"AWS_REGION\", \"ap-south-1\")\n\n    if aws_access_key_id and aws_secret_access_key:\n        return boto3.client(\n            \"s3\",\n            aws_access_key_id=aws_access_key_id,\n            aws_secret_access_key=aws_secret_access_key,\n            region_name=aws_region,\n        )\n\n    return boto3.client(\"s3\", region_name=aws_region)",

        "contents": "def _run_pense_voice_simulation(\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"Voice simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n\n\n        ...\n\n\n        # Upload audios folder for this simulation to S3\n        if audios_dir.exists() and audios_dir.is_dir():\n            audios_s3_prefix = f\"{s3_prefix}/{sim_name}/audios\"\n            audio_files_uploaded = 0\n            for audio_file in audios_dir.iterdir():\n                if audio_file.is_file() and (\n                    audio_file.suffix == \".wav\"\n                    or audio_file.suffix == \".mp3\"\n                    or audio_file.suffix == \".ogg\"\n                ):\n                    relative_audio_path = audio_file.relative_to(output_dir)\n                    audio_s3_key = f\"{s3_prefix}/{relative_audio_path}\"\n                    s3.upload_file(str(audio_file), s3_bucket, audio_s3_key)\n                    uploaded_audio_files.add(str(audio_file))\n                    audio_files_uploaded += 1\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Uploaded audio file {audio_file.name} to S3: {audio_s3_key}\"\n                    )\n            if audio_files_uploaded \u003e 0:\n                audios_s3_paths[sim_name] = f\"s3://{s3_bucket}/{audios_s3_prefix}\"\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Uploaded {audio_files_uploaded} audio file(s) for {sim_name} to {audios_s3_paths[sim_name]}\"\n                )\n\n        simulation_results.append(\n            {\n                \"simulation_name\": sim_name,\n                \"persona\": persona_data,\n                \"scenario\": scenario_data,\n                \"evaluation_results\": eval_results,\n                \"transcript\": transcript,\n                \"audios_s3_path\": audios_s3_paths.get(sim_name),\n            }\n        )",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\n\n        # Upload audios folder for this simulation to S3\n        if audios_dir.exists() and audios_dir.is_dir():\n            audios_s3_prefix = f\"{s3_prefix}/{sim_name}/audios\"\n            audio_files_uploaded = 0\n            for audio_file in audios_dir.iterdir():\n                if audio_file.is_file() and (\n                    audio_file.suffix == \".wav\"\n                    or audio_file.suffix == \".mp3\"\n                    or audio_file.suffix == \".ogg\"\n                ):\n                    relative_audio_path = audio_file.relative_to(output_dir)\n                    audio_s3_key = f\"{s3_prefix}/{relative_audio_path}\"\n                    s3.upload_file(str(audio_file), s3_bucket, audio_s3_key)\n                    uploaded_audio_files.add(str(audio_file))\n                    audio_files_uploaded += 1\n                    logger.info(\n                        f\"Uploaded audio file {audio_file.name} to S3: {audio_s3_key}\"\n                    )\n            if audio_files_uploaded \u003e 0:\n                audios_s3_paths[sim_name] = f\"s3://{s3_bucket}/{audios_s3_prefix}\"\n                logger.info(\n                    f\"Uploaded {audio_files_uploaded} audio file(s) for {sim_name} to {audios_s3_paths[sim_name]}\"\n                )\n\n        simulation_results.append(\n            {\n                \"simulation_name\": sim_name,\n                \"persona\": persona_data,\n                \"scenario\": scenario_data,\n                \"evaluation_results\": eval_results,\n                \"transcript\": transcript,\n                \"audios_s3_path\": audios_s3_paths.get(sim_name),\n            }\n        )",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{simulation_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a simulation with personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM simulations command\n    with the agent's config and the simulation's personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Uses the agent linked to the simulation and its LLM model configuration.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Simulation not found\")\n\n    # Get agent from simulation\n    agent_uuid = simulation.get(\"agent_id\")\n    if not agent_uuid:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"No agent linked to this simulation. Link an agent to the simulation first.\",\n        )\n\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Get linked entities\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no personas. Add at least one persona.\",\n        )\n\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no scenarios. Add at least one scenario.\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))",
        "detailedLines": [

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/{simulation_uuid}/run\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_simulation_endpoint(simulation_uuid: str, request: RunSimulationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a simulation with personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM simulations command\n    with the agent's config and the simulation's personas, scenarios, and metrics.\n\n    Uses the agent linked to the simulation and its LLM model configuration.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify simulation exists\n    simulation = get_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    if not simulation:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Simulation not found\")\n\n    # Get agent from simulation\n    agent_uuid = simulation.get(\"agent_id\")\n    if not agent_uuid:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"No agent linked to this simulation. Link an agent to the simulation first.\",\n        )\n\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    # Get linked entities\n    personas = get_personas_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    scenarios = get_scenarios_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n    metrics = get_metrics_for_simulation(simulation_uuid)\n\n    if not personas:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no personas. Add at least one persona.\",\n        )\n\n    if not scenarios:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Simulation has no scenarios. Add at least one scenario.\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\napp = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)\n\n# Include routers\napp.include_router(agents_router)\napp.include_router(tools_router)\napp.include_router(agent_tools_router)\napp.include_router(stt_router)\napp.include_router(tts_router)\napp.include_router(tests_router)\napp.include_router(agent_tests_router)\napp.include_router(personas_router)\napp.include_router(scenarios_router)\napp.include_router(metrics_router)\napp.include_router(simulations_router)\n\napp.add_middleware(\n    CORSMiddleware,\n    allow_origins=[\"*\"],  # Allow all origins\n    allow_credentials=True,\n    allow_methods=[\"*\"],\n    allow_headers=[\"*\"],\n)\n\n\nclass PresignedURLRequest(BaseModel):\n    task_type: Literal[\"stt\", \"tts\", \"agent\"]\n    content_type: str  # e.g., \"audio/wav\", \"text/csv\"\n    extension: str  # e.g., \"wav\", \"csv\"",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "lineNumber": 36,
            "text": "@asynccontextmanager"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 37,
            "text": "async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 41
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 42,
            "text": "    recover_pending_jobs()"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 43,
            "text": "    yield"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 44,
            "text": "    # Shutdown: Nothing to do for now"
          },
          {
            "isSignature": true,
            "lineNumber": 45,
            "text": "    logger.info(\"Application shutting down\")"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 46
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 47
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 48,
            "text": "app = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)"

            "lineNumber": 49
          },

            "lineNumber": 50,
            "text": "# Include routers"
          },
          {
            "lineNumber": 51,
            "text": "app.include_router(agents_router)"

            "lineNumber": 52,

        ],
        "originalContents": "\n\n\napp = FastAPI(lifespan=lifespan)\n\n# Include routers\napp.include_router(agents_router)\napp.include_router(tools_router)\napp.include_router(agent_tools_router)\napp.include_router(stt_router)\napp.include_router(tts_router)\napp.include_router(tests_router)\napp.include_router(agent_tests_router)\napp.include_router(personas_router)\napp.include_router(scenarios_router)\napp.include_router(metrics_router)\napp.include_router(simulations_router)\n\napp.add_middleware(\n    CORSMiddleware,\n    allow_origins=[\"*\"],  # Allow all origins\n    allow_credentials=True,\n    allow_methods=[\"*\"],\n    allow_headers=[\"*\"],\n)\n\n\nclass PresignedURLRequest(BaseModel):\n    task_type: Literal[\"stt\", \"tts\", \"agent\"]\n    content_type: str  # e.g., \"audio/wav\", \"text/csv\"\n    extension: str  # e.g., \"wav\", \"csv\"",

          },
          "startPosition": {
            "line": 35
          }

        "contents": "def _run_pense_chat_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n\n                ...\n\n                if transcript_file.exists():\n                    try:\n                        with open(transcript_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                            transcript = json.load(f)\n                    except Exception as e:\n                        logger.warning(\n                            f\"Failed to parse transcript.json for {sim_name}: {e}\"\n                        )\n\n                # Parse config.json to get persona and scenario data\n                persona_data = None\n                scenario_data = None\n                if config_file.exists():\n                    try:\n                        with open(config_file, \"r\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n                            config_data = json.load(f)\n                            persona_data = config_data.get(\"persona\")\n                            scenario_data = config_data.get(\"scenario\")\n                    except Exception as e:\n                        logger.warning(\n                            f\"Failed to parse config.json for {sim_name}: {e}\"\n                        )\n\n                simulation_results.append(\n                    {\n                        \"simulation_name\": sim_name,\n                        \"persona\": persona_data,\n                        \"scenario\": scenario_data,\n                        \"evaluation_results\": eval_results,\n                        \"transcript\": transcript,\n                    }\n                )\n\n    # Upload results to S3\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(output_dir):\n        for file in files:\n            local_file_path = Path(root) / file\n            relative_path = local_file_path.relative_to(output_dir)\n            s3_key = f\"{s3_prefix}/{relative_path}\"\n            s3.upload_file(str(local_file_path), s3_bucket, s3_key)\n\n    error = None\n    if result.returncode != 0:\n        error = f\"Command failed: {result.stderr}\"",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/agent/{agent_uuid}/benchmark\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_agent_benchmark(agent_uuid: str, request: BenchmarkRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a benchmark comparing multiple models on the same tests.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM tests command\n    for each model in parallel, then generates a leaderboard comparing results.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    if not request.test_uuids:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400, detail=\"At least one test UUID is required\"\n        )\n\n    if not request.models:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=\"At least one model is required\")\n\n    # Verify all tests exist and are linked to the agent\n    tests = []\n    for test_uuid in request.test_uuids:\n        test = get_test(test_uuid)\n        if not test:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} not found\")\n\n        link = get_agent_test_link(agent_uuid, test_uuid)\n        if not link:\n            raise HTTPException(\n                status_code=400,\n                detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} is not linked to this agent. Link the test first.\",\n            )\n\n        tests.append(test)\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_agent_test_job(\n        agent_id=agent_uuid,\n        job_type=\"llm-benchmark\",\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"agent_uuid\": agent_uuid,\n            \"test_uuids\": request.test_uuids,\n            \"models\": request.models,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/agent/{agent_uuid}/benchmark\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def run_agent_benchmark(agent_uuid: str, request: BenchmarkRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Run a benchmark comparing multiple models on the same tests.\n\n    This starts a background task that runs the pense LLM tests command\n    for each model in parallel, then generates a leaderboard comparing results.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Verify agent exists\n    agent = get_agent(agent_uuid)\n    if not agent:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=\"Agent not found\")\n\n    if not request.test_uuids:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400, detail=\"At least one test UUID is required\"\n        )\n\n    if not request.models:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=\"At least one model is required\")\n\n    # Verify all tests exist and are linked to the agent\n    tests = []\n    for test_uuid in request.test_uuids:\n        test = get_test(test_uuid)\n        if not test:\n            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} not found\")\n\n        link = get_agent_test_link(agent_uuid, test_uuid)\n        if not link:\n            raise HTTPException(\n                status_code=400,\n                detail=f\"Test {test_uuid} is not linked to this agent. Link the test first.\",\n            )\n\n        tests.append(test)\n\n    # Get S3 configuration\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_agent_test_job(\n        agent_id=agent_uuid,\n        job_type=\"llm-benchmark\",\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"agent_uuid\": agent_uuid,\n            \"test_uuids\": request.test_uuids,\n            \"models\": request.models,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )",

        "contents": "\n\n\ndef run_evaluation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    request: STTEvaluationRequest,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the STT evaluation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running evaluation task {task_id} with {len(request.providers)} providers\"\n        )\n        update_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        # Create temporary directory for processing\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as temp_dir:\n            temp_path = Path(temp_dir)\n\n            try:\n                # Create directory structure\n                input_dir = temp_path / \"input\"\n                input_dir.mkdir()\n                audios_wav_dir = input_dir / \"audios\" / \"wav\"\n                audios_pcm16_dir = input_dir / \"audios\" / \"pcm16\"\n                audios_wav_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n                audios_pcm16_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n\n                # Download audio files from S3 and create CSV\n                stt_csv_path = input_dir / \"stt.csv\"",

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef run_evaluation_task(\n    task_id: str,\n    request: STTEvaluationRequest,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n):\n    \"\"\"Run the STT evaluation in the background.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        logger.info(\n            f\"Running evaluation task {task_id} with {len(request.providers)} providers\"\n        )\n        update_job(task_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)\n\n        s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n        # Create temporary directory for processing\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as temp_dir:\n            temp_path = Path(temp_dir)\n\n            try:\n                # Create directory structure\n                input_dir = temp_path / \"input\"\n                input_dir.mkdir()\n                audios_wav_dir = input_dir / \"audios\" / \"wav\"\n                audios_pcm16_dir = input_dir / \"audios\" / \"pcm16\"\n                audios_wav_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n                audios_pcm16_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n\n                # Download audio files from S3 and create CSV\n                stt_csv_path = input_dir / \"stt.csv\"",
        "range": {

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/evaluate\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def evaluate_stt(request: STTEvaluationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Start a background task to evaluate multiple STT providers with audio files from S3.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Validate input\n    if len(request.audio_paths) != len(request.texts):\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Number of audio paths must match number of ground truth texts\",\n        )\n\n    if not request.providers:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"At least one provider must be specified\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration from environment\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_job(\n        job_type=\"stt-eval\",\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"audio_paths\": request.audio_paths,\n            \"texts\": request.texts,\n            \"providers\": request.providers,\n            \"language\": request.language,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )\n\n    # Start background task in a separate thread\n    thread = threading.Thread(\n        target=run_evaluation_task,\n        args=(job_id, request, s3_bucket),\n        daemon=True,\n    )\n    thread.start()\n\n    return TaskCreateResponse(task_id=job_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)",
        "detailedLines": [
          {
            "isSignature": true,

            "isSignature": true,

          },

            "isSignature": true,

          },

            "text": "):"

            "lineNumber": 409
          },

        "originalContents": "\n\n\n@router.post(\"/evaluate\", response_model=TaskCreateResponse)\nasync def evaluate_stt(request: STTEvaluationRequest):\n    \"\"\"\n    Start a background task to evaluate multiple STT providers with audio files from S3.\n\n    Returns a task ID that can be used to poll for status and results.\n    \"\"\"\n    # Validate input\n    if len(request.audio_paths) != len(request.texts):\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"Number of audio paths must match number of ground truth texts\",\n        )\n\n    if not request.providers:\n        raise HTTPException(\n            status_code=400,\n            detail=\"At least one provider must be specified\",\n        )\n\n    # Get S3 configuration from environment\n    try:\n        s3_bucket = get_s3_output_config()\n    except ValueError as e:\n        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))\n\n    # Create job in database with details for recovery\n    job_id = create_job(\n        job_type=\"stt-eval\",\n        status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value,\n        details={\n            \"audio_paths\": request.audio_paths,\n            \"texts\": request.texts,\n            \"providers\": request.providers,\n            \"language\": request.language,\n            \"s3_bucket\": s3_bucket,\n        },\n        results=None,\n    )\n\n    # Start background task in a separate thread\n    thread = threading.Thread(\n        target=run_evaluation_task,\n        args=(job_id, request, s3_bucket),\n        daemon=True,\n    )\n    thread.start()\n\n    return TaskCreateResponse(task_id=job_id, status=TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS.value)",

    {
      "codeBlock": {
        "contents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_chat_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"",
        "detailedLines": [
          {

        "originalContents": "\n\n\ndef _run_pense_chat_simulation(\n    model: str,\n    pense_config: Dict[str, Any],\n    output_dir: Path,\n    s3_bucket: str,\n    s3_prefix: str,\n    log_prefix: str = \"LLM simulation\",\n) -\u003e Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Run pense llm simulations run command and return parsed results.\n\n    Args:\n        model: Model name to use\n        pense_config: The pense config dict\n        output_dir: Directory to write output files\n        s3_bucket: S3 bucket name\n        s3_prefix: S3 key prefix for uploading results\n        log_prefix: Prefix for log messages\n\n    Returns:\n        Dict with keys: success, total_simulations, metrics, simulation_results, error\n    \"\"\"\n    s3 = get_s3_client()\n\n    # Update config with model\n    config = pense_config.copy()\n    config[\"params\"] = {\"model\": model}\n\n    # Resolve output directory to absolute path\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\n\n    # Create output directory\n    output_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\n\n    # Write config to temp file\n    config_file_name = \"simulation_config\"\n    config_file = output_dir / f\"{config_file_name}.json\"\n    with open(config_file, \"w\", encoding=\"utf-8\") as f:\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\n\n    # Run pense llm simulations run command\n    # Use absolute paths for config and output\n    run_cmd = [\n        \"pense\",\n        \"llm\",\n        \"simulations\",\n        \"run\",\n        \"-c\",\n        str(config_file),\n        \"-o\",\n        str(output_dir),\n        \"-m\",\n        model,\n    ]\n\n    logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} command: {' '.join(run_cmd)}\")\n\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        run_cmd,\n        capture_output=True,\n        text=True,\n        cwd=str(output_dir),\n    )\n\n    if result.stdout:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stdout: {result.stdout}\")\n    if result.stderr:\n        logger.info(f\"{log_prefix} stderr: {result.stderr}\")\n\n    # Parse results\n    metrics_data = None\n    results_data = None\n    simulation_results = []\n\n    # Find metrics.json and results.csv files\n    metrics_file = output_dir / \"metrics.json\"",

Adding a helper function to list S3 audio files and generate presigned URLs, then updating the endpoint:


_**Agent (composer-1)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="search_replace">

<details><summary>Tool use: **search_replace** • Edit file: src/routers/simulations.py</summary>

**Chunk 1**
Lines added: 107, lines removed: 0

```diff
@@ -48,4 +48,111 @@
  router = APIRouter(prefix="/simulations", tags=["simulations"])
  
+ 
+ def _get_audio_urls_from_s3_path(s3_path: str, s3_bucket: str) -&gt; List[str]:
+     """
+     List all audio files in an S3 path and generate presigned URLs for them.
+     
+     Args:
+         s3_path: S3 path in format s3://bucket/key or bucket/key
+         s3_bucket: S3 bucket name
+         
+     Returns:
+         List of presigned URLs for audio files, sorted by filename
+     """
+     try:
+         s3 = get_s3_client()
+         
+         # Parse S3 path
+         if s3_path.startswith("s3://"):
+             # Remove s3:// prefix and bucket name
+             path_without_prefix = s3_path[5:]  # Remove "s3://"
+             if path_without_prefix.startswith(f"{s3_bucket}/"):
+                 s3_prefix = path_without_prefix[len(f"{s3_bucket}/"):]
+             else:
+                 # If bucket doesn't match, assume the whole thing after s3:// is the key
+                 parts = path_without_prefix.split("/", 1)
+                 if len(parts) &gt; 1:
+                     s3_prefix = parts[1]
+                 else:
+                     s3_prefix = path_without_prefix
+         else:
+             # Assume it's already just the key/prefix
+             s3_prefix = s3_path
+         
+         # Ensure prefix ends with / for directory listing
+         if s3_prefix and not s3_prefix.endswith("/"):
+             s3_prefix += "/"
+         
+         # List objects in the S3 prefix
+         audio_extensions = {".wav", ".mp3", ".ogg"}
+         audio_files = []
+         
+         paginator = s3.get_paginator("list_objects_v2")
+         for page in paginator.paginate(Bucket=s3_bucket, Prefix=s3_prefix):
+             if "Contents" in page:
+                 for obj in page["Contents"]:
+                     key = obj["Key"]
+                     # Skip if it's a directory marker
+                     if key.endswith("/"):
+                         continue
+                     # Check if it's an audio file
+                     file_ext = Path(key).suffix.lower()
+                     if file_ext in audio_extensions:
+                         audio_files.append(key)
+         
+         # Sort audio files by filename (natural sort for numbered files)
+         def natural_sort_key(key: str) -&gt; tuple:
+             """Extract numeric parts for natural sorting"""
+             filename = Path(key).name
+             parts = []
+             current_num = ""
+             current_str = ""
+             for char in filename:
+                 if char.isdigit():
+                     current_num += char
+                     if current_str:
+                         parts.append((0, current_str))
+                         current_str = ""
+                 else:
+                     if current_num:
+                         parts.append((1, int(current_num)))
+                         current_num = ""
+                     current_str += char
+             if current_num:
+                 parts.append((1, int(current_num)))
+             if current_str:
+                 parts.append((0, current_str))
+             return tuple(parts)
+         
+         audio_files.sort(key=natural_sort_key)
+         
+         # Generate presigned URLs
+         expiration = 3600  # 1 hour expiration
+         presigned_urls = []
+         for audio_key in audio_files:
+             try:
+                 presigned_url = s3.generate_presigned_url(
+                     "get_object",
+                     Params={
+                         "Bucket": s3_bucket,
+                         "Key": audio_key,
+                     },
+                     ExpiresIn=expiration,
+                 )
+                 presigned_urls.append(presigned_url)
+                 logger.info(f"Generated presigned URL for {audio_key}")
+             except Exception as e:
+                 logger.warning(
+                     f"Failed to generate presigned URL for {audio_key}: {str(e)}"
+                 )
+                 # Fallback to S3 path if presigned URL generation fails
+                 presigned_urls.append(f"s3://{s3_bucket}/{audio_key}")
+         
+         return presigned_urls
+     
+     except Exception as e:
+         logger.error(f"Error listing audio files from S3 path {s3_path}: {str(e)}")
+         return []
+ 
  
  class SimulationCreate(BaseModel):
```
